Chapter 1.1
Part 1 — Programming Foundations

Programming Basics

"Code likhna seekhne se pehle, programming soochna seekho."
Programming ek tarika hai computer ko step-by-step instructions dene ka taaki woh koi kaam kar sake. Jaise tum ek recipe follow karte ho, computer bhi exactly waisi hi instructions follow karta hai — na zyada, na kam. Yeh chapter programming ki sabse zaroori building blocks cover karta hai jo AI agent development ke har step mein kaam aate hain.

1.1.1 Programming Kya Hai?

📖 Definition
Programming ek process hai jisme hum ek programming language ka use karke computer ko specific tasks perform karne ke liye instructions likhte hain. Yeh instructions collectively ek program ya software kehlaate hain.

Ek AI agent likhna ho ya ek simple calculator — dono mein yahi basic flow hoti hai:

Diagram 1.1 — Programming ka Basic Flow
Problem
(real world)
→
Algorithm
(logic design)
→
Code
(language mein)
→
Run & Test
(computer pe)
→
Output
(result)

1.1.2 Compiler vs Interpreter

Code ko computer nahi samajhta — use pehle machine language mein translate karna padta hai. Yeh kaam do tarike se hota hai:

FeatureCompilerInterpreter
Kaise kaam karta haiPura code ek saath translate karta haiLine by line translate karta hai
SpeedFast (already compiled)Slow (real-time translate)
Error kab milta haiCompilation ke baadExecution ke time
ExamplesC, C++, Rust, GoPython, JavaScript, Ruby
AI/ML usePerformance-critical partsRapid prototyping, ML models
💬 Hinglish mein samjho
Compiler ek translator jaisa hai jo pehle poora book translate karta hai, phir publish karta hai. Interpreter ek simultaneous interpreter jaisa hai jo sentence-by-sentence translate karta hai — jaise UN mein hota hai.

1.1.3 Variables — Data Store Karna

📖 Definition
Variable ek named memory location hai jisme program data store karta hai. Name ek label hai, value actual data hai.
Diagram 1.2 — Variable Memory Model
0x001A
25
age
0x001B
"Alice"
name
0x001C
3.14
pi
0x001D
True
is_active

RAM mein har variable ek address pe store hota hai. Python ye automatically manage karta hai.

## Variable Declaration in Python

# Integer variable
age = 25

# String variable
name = "Alice"

# Float variable
salary = 50000.50

# Boolean variable
is_agent_running = True

# Multiple assignment
x, y, z = 1, 2, 3

# Constant (convention: UPPER_CASE)
MAX_TOKENS = 4096

print(f"Agent {name} is {age} years old, max tokens: {MAX_TOKENS}")
Python

Naming Rules (Variable Name ke Rules)

✅ Sahi Naamkaran
user_name, _private, score2, myAgent, MAX_SIZE
❌ Galat Naamkaran
2score (digit se shuru), my-var (hyphen), for (keyword), my var (space)
3

1.1.4 Operators — Kaam Karna Data pe

Operators ek kaam karte hain: ek ya zyada operands pe operation perform karte hain aur ek result return karte hain. AI agents mein operators har jagah hain — scoring, comparison, boolean logic, string concatenation.

TypeOperatorsExampleResult
Arithmetic+ - * / // % **10 // 33 (floor div)
Comparison== != > < >= <=5 >= 3True
Logicaland or notTrue and FalseFalse
Assignment= += -= *= /=x += 5x = x+5
Identityis is notx is NoneTrue/False
Membershipin not in"a" in "agent"True
Bitwise& | ^ ~ << >>5 & 31
⚡ AI Agent mein Operator Use
Comparison operators → tool selection logic mein. Logical operators → guardrail conditions mein. Membership in → context window check mein. Modulo % → rate limiting, pagination mein.

1.1.5 Input aur Output

Input

User se ya file se data lena. Python mein input(); real AI apps mein API request body ya file upload.

name = input("Apna naam batao: ")
print(f"Hello, {name}!")
Python

Output

Screen pe ya file mein result dikhana. print() basic hai; production mein logging module use karo.

import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logging.info("Agent started")
print("Result:", 42)
Python

1.1.6 Keywords — Reserved Words

Keywords woh words hain jo Python ne apne liye reserve kar rakhe hain. Inhe variable names ke roop mein use nahi kar sakte.

🧠 Python Keywords (35 total)
False True None and or not if elif else for while break continue pass return def class import from as try except finally raise with lambda yield del global nonlocal in is

1.1.7 Type Conversion — Data Ka Roop Badalna

📖 Definition
Type Conversion ek data type ke value ko doosre data type mein convert karna hai. Do types hote hain: Implicit (Python khud karta hai) aur Explicit (hum manually karte hain).
Diagram 1.3 — Type Conversion Paths
int()→ 42
float, str, bool se
float()→ 3.14
int, str se
str()→ "42"
any type se
bool()→ True
0 = False, else True

⚠️ Incorrect type conversion se ValueError aata hai — production mein hamesha try/except lagao.

# Explicit Type Conversion — AI Agent context

user_input = "100"          # API se aaya string
tokens = int(user_input)    # int mein convert karo

temperature = "0.7"
temp_float = float(temperature)

# Safe conversion — production best practice
def safe_int(value, default=0):
    try:
        return int(value)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default

result = safe_int("abc")   # Returns 0 instead of crashing
Python

1.1.8 String Formatting — Text ka Jugaad

AI agents mein prompts, responses, logs — sab kuch text hai. String formatting ek core skill hai.

MethodExampleKab Use Karein
f-strings (best)f"Hello {name}"Har jagah — fastest & readable
.format()"Hello {}".format(name)Dynamic templates mein
% (old)"Hello %s" % nameLegacy code mein
Template stringsTemplate("$name")User input se templates mein (safer)
# AI Agent ke liye prompt template
system_prompt = """
You are a helpful assistant.
User query: {query}
Max tokens: {max_tokens}
Temperature: {temp}
"""

# f-string version — production mein prefer karo
query = "What is RAG?"
max_tokens = 512
temp = 0.7

prompt = f"""You are a helpful assistant.
User query: {query}
Max tokens: {max_tokens}
Temperature: {temp}"""

print(prompt)
Python
📝 Chapter 1.1 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek variable agent_name banao jisme apna naam ho. Ek variable task_count banao jisme 5 ho. Dono ko f-string se print karo.
  2. User se ek number input lo, usse int mein convert karo, aur uska square print karo. Safe conversion use karo.
  3. Ek prompt template banao jo model_name, temperature, aur system_instruction le aur ek formatted string return kare.
  4. Ek expression likhao: agar tokens 512 se zyada ho aur model "gpt-4" ho, to True return karo. Logical operators use karo.
5
Chapter 1.2

Data Types

"Data ka type pata hoga toh kya karna hai woh bhi pata hoga."
Data types Python ko batate hain ki ek value ke saath kya operation kiya ja sakta hai aur woh value memory mein kaise store hogi. AI agent mein text, numbers, booleans, lists, dictionaries — sabka different role hai. Is chapter mein har type ko deeply samjhenge.

1.2.1 Primitive Data Types

Integer (int)

📖 Definition
Integer ek poora number hai — koi decimal nahi. Positive, negative, ya zero. Python mein integers ka size unlimited hai (memory ke hisaab se). 
age = 25
negative = -100
big_num = 1_000_000   # Underscore readability ke liye
hex_val = 0xFF          # Hexadecimal → 255
bin_val = 0b1010        # Binary → 10
print(type(age))         # <class 'int'>
Python

Float (float)

Decimal numbers. AI mein temperature, probability scores, loss values sab floats hain.

temp = 0.7
pi = 3.14159
scientific = 1.5e-3    # = 0.0015 (scientific notation)

# Float precision issue — ye jaanna zaroori hai!
print(0.1 + 0.2)        # 0.30000000000000004 ← floating point error!
from decimal import Decimal
print(Decimal('0.1') + Decimal('0.2'))  # 0.3 ← sahi
Python
⚠️ Float Precision Warning
AI models mein confidence scores compare karte waqt round() ya math.isclose() use karo — direct == mat karo floats pe.

Boolean (bool)

Sirf True ya False. Internally int ka subclass hai — True == 1, False == 0.

is_running = True
has_memory = False

# Truthy / Falsy values — AI mein bahut important!
# Falsy: False, 0, 0.0, "", [], {}, None
# Truthy: Everything else

context = []  # empty list
if context:
    print("Context hai")
else:
    print("Context empty hai — retrieve karo")
Python

String (str)

Text data. AI engineering mein sab kuch string se start hota hai — user query, prompt, LLM output, tool result. String ek immutable sequence of characters hai.

# String creation
single = 'Hello'
double = "World"
multi = """Yeh ek
multiline string hai"""

# Important string operations
s = "AI Agent Engineering"
print(s.upper())           # AI AGENT ENGINEERING
print(s.lower())           # ai agent engineering
print(s.split())           # ['AI', 'Agent', 'Engineering']
print(s.replace("AI", "ML")) # ML Agent Engineering
print(len(s))              # 20

# Slicing — prompt engineering mein use hota hai
print(s[0:8])             # "AI Agent"
print(s[-11:])            # "Engineering"
Python
7

1.2.2 Collection Data Types

List (list) — Ordered, Mutable

📖 Definition
List ek ordered collection hai jo multiple values store karta hai. Mutable hai — matlab change ho sakta hai. AI mein: conversation history, search results, tool names, retrieved chunks. 
tools = ["search", "calculator", "browser"]
tools.append("email")    # Add at end
tools.insert(0, "memory") # Add at index 0
tools.remove("browser") # Remove by value
print(tools[-1])         # Last element: "email"

# Conversation history (AI agent use case)
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "Hello"},
    {"role": "assistant", "content": "Hi! How can I help?"}
]
messages.append({"role": "user", "content": "What is RAG?"})
Python

Tuple (tuple) — Ordered, Immutable

List jaisa hai par change nahi ho sakta. Use karo jab data constant rakhna ho — jaise coordinates, RGB colors, config settings.

model_config = ("gpt-4", 0.7, 512)  # (name, temp, max_tokens)
model, temp, tokens = model_config  # Tuple unpacking
print(model)  # gpt-4
Python

Set (set) — Unordered, Unique Values

Duplicate values automatically remove ho jaate hain. Set operations (union, intersection) bahut fast hain — O(1) lookup.

allowed_tools = {"search", "calculator", "email"}
requested = {"calculator", "browser"}

# Kaunse tools allowed hain?
valid = allowed_tools & requested      # Intersection: {'calculator'}
blocked = requested - allowed_tools    # Difference: {'browser'}
print(f"Valid: {valid}, Blocked: {blocked}")
Python

Dictionary (dict) — Key-Value Pairs

📖 Definition
Dictionary key-value pairs store karta hai. Keys unique honi chahiye. AI agent ka state, tool arguments, API responses — sab dict mein aate hain. 
agent_state = {
    "name": "ResearchBot",
    "tools": ["search", "summarize"],
    "memory": [],
    "iteration": 0,
    "is_complete": False
}

# Access, update, delete
print(agent_state["name"])          # ResearchBot
agent_state["iteration"] += 1      # Update
agent_state.get("timeout", 30)     # Safe access with default

# Dict comprehension — tools ko filter karo
tool_status = {k: True for k in agent_state["tools"]}
Python

1.2.3 None — Kuch Nahi Ka Value

None Python mein "koi value nahi" represent karta hai. Null, nil, undefined — yahi sab ka equivalent hai Python mein.

1.2.4 Mutable vs Immutable

Diagram 1.4 — Mutable vs Immutable Types
✅ Mutable (Change ho sakta)

list, dict, set, bytearray

AI use: conversation history, agent state, retrieved chunks

🔒 Immutable (Change nahi ho sakta)

int, float, bool, str, tuple, frozenset

AI use: config values, model name, API keys (should be constant)

⚡ Performance Tip
Loop mein string concatenation (s += "text") mat karo — ye har baar new string banata hai (O(n²)). Bajaye iske list.append() karo phir "".join() karo — ye O(n) hai. Prompt building mein yeh matter karta hai.
📝 Chapter 1.2 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek list banao jisme 5 AI tools ke naam ho. append, remove, aur sort karke print karo.
  2. Ek agent_config dict banao jisme model, temperature, max_tokens, tools keys ho. .get() se safely values access karo.
  3. Ek string ko split() karke words ki list banao, phir duplicates hataane ke liye set() mein convert karo.
  4. Float precision problem demonstrate karo: 0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3 check karo aur explain karo result kyon aisa hai.
9
Chapter 1.3

Control Flow

"Program ko batao: kab ruko, kab chalo, kab ulto wapas jao."
Control flow wo mechanism hai jisse program decide karta hai ki kaun si line execute hogi aur kitni baar. Without control flow, program ek straight line mein chalta — koi decision nahi, koi loop nahi. AI agents ki decision making, retry logic, tool selection — sab control flow pe based hai.

1.3.1 Conditional Statements — if / elif / else

Diagram 1.5 — if/elif/else Flowchart
Start
↓
Condition 1
if tokens < 100
TRUE
↙
FALSE
↘
Block 1
retrieve more
Condition 2
elif tokens > 4000
↓               ↓
Continue
Truncate
else: proceed
# AI Agent mein tool selection logic
def decide_action(user_query, context_tokens):
    if context_tokens > 3500:
        return "summarize_context"
    elif "search" in user_query.lower():
        return "web_search"
    elif "calculate" in user_query.lower():
        return "calculator"
    else:
        return "respond_directly"

action = decide_action("search for RAG papers", 1200)
print(action)  # web_search
Python

1.3.2 Loops — Kaam Baar Baar Karna

for Loop

Kisi sequence (list, string, range, dict) ke har element pe operation karo.

tools = ["search", "summarize", "email"]
for i, tool in enumerate(tools):
    print(f"{i+1}. Tool: {tool}")

# Agent retry loop
for attempt in range(3):    # 0, 1, 2
    print(f"Attempt {attempt+1}/3")

# Dict loop
config = {"model": "gpt-4", "temp": 0.7}
for key, value in config.items():
    print(f"{key}: {value}")
Python

while Loop

Jab tak condition True ho, chalta rahe. AI agent ka main execution loop yahi hota hai!

# AI Agent main loop — while loop ka perfect example
max_iterations = 10
iteration = 0
task_complete = False

while not task_complete and iteration < max_iterations:
    iteration += 1
    print(f"Agent iteration {iteration}")
    # action = agent.step()
    # if action == "FINISH": task_complete = True
    task_complete = (iteration == 3)   # Simulated

print(f"Done in {iteration} iterations")
Python

break, continue, pass

KeywordKya karta haiUse case
breakLoop turant band karta haiAnswer mil gaya — loop band karo
continueCurrent iteration skip karta haiInvalid input skip karo, next pe jao
passKuch nahi karta (placeholder)Empty class/function ka syntax fill

1.3.3 Iterators aur Generators

Iterator

Object jo __iter__ aur __next__ implement karta hai. for loop internally iterator use karta hai.

Generator — Memory Efficient!

yield use karta hai. Lazy evaluation — sab data ek saath memory mein nahi laata. Large datasets ke liye perfect.

# Generator — large document processing ke liye
def chunk_text(text, size=512):
    """Text ko chunks mein yield karo — memory efficient"""
    for i in range(0, len(text), size):
        yield text[i:i+size]

document = "A" * 5000  # 5000 character document
for chunk in chunk_text(document):
    print(f"Processing chunk of {len(chunk)} chars")
Python
📝 Chapter 1.3 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek while loop likho jo agent ke iterations count kare aur max 5 pe rok le.
  2. for loop se ek list of numbers ka sum nikalo — sum() built-in use mat karo.
  3. Ek generator fibonacci(n) banao jo n Fibonacci numbers yield kare.
  4. Ek if/elif/else chain banao jo LLM response ko classify kare: "error", "empty", "too_long" (>2000 chars), "normal".
12
Chapter 1.4

Functions

"Code ko reusable banao — ek baar likho, baar baar use karo."
Function ek reusable block of code hai jise ek naam diya gaya hai. Jab bhi wo kaam karna ho, function ka naam leke call karo. AI agent mein har operation — retrieve, summarize, call_tool, format_prompt — ek function hona chahiye. Yahi clean code ka pehla rule hai.

1.4.1 Function Declaration aur Call

# Basic function — input leta hai, output return karta hai
def format_prompt(query: str, context: str = "") -> str:
    """
    User query aur context ko combine karke prompt banao.

    Args:
        query: User ka sawal
        context: Retrieved knowledge (optional)
    Returns:
        Formatted prompt string
    """
    if context:
        return f"Context:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {query}"
    return f"Question: {query}"

# Function call
prompt = format_prompt("What is RAG?", "RAG means Retrieval Augmented Generation...")
print(prompt)
Python

1.4.2 Parameters ke Types

TypeSyntaxExample
Positionaldef f(a, b)f(1, 2)
Defaultdef f(a, b=10)f(5) → b=10
Keyworddef f(a, b)f(b=2, a=1)
*argsdef f(*args)Variable positional args
**kwargsdef f(**kwargs)Variable keyword args (dict)
# *args aur **kwargs — agent tool caller mein use hota hai
def call_tool(tool_name: str, *args, **kwargs):
    print(f"Calling tool: {tool_name}")
    print(f"Positional args: {args}")
    print(f"Keyword args: {kwargs}")

call_tool("web_search", "RAG", "2024", max_results=5, lang="en")
Python

1.4.3 Lambda Functions

Ek-line anonymous functions. Filtering, sorting, mapping mein use hoti hain.

# Sort tool results by score
results = [
    {"text": "RAG intro", "score": 0.87},
    {"text": "RAG advanced", "score": 0.94},
    {"text": "Vector DBs", "score": 0.76}
]
sorted_results = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x["score"], reverse=True)
print(sorted_results[0]["text"])  # RAG advanced
Python

1.4.4 Recursion

📖 Definition
Recursion tab hoti hai jab ek function khud ko call karta hai. Har recursive call ek smaller problem solve karta hai. Ek base case zaroori hai — warna infinite loop. 
# Recursive agent — task decompose karo sub-tasks mein
def decompose_task(task: str, depth: int = 0, max_depth: int = 3):
    indent = "  " * depth
    print(f"{indent}Task: {task}")
    if depth >= max_depth:              # Base case — ruk jao
        print(f"{indent}→ Execute directly")
        return
    # Sub-tasks (simplified for demo)
    subtasks = [f"{task}_step{i}" for i in range(1, 3)]
    for sub in subtasks:
        decompose_task(sub, depth + 1, max_depth)  # Recursive call

decompose_task("Research AI agents")
Python

1.4.5 Closures aur Decorators

Closure

Inner function jo outer function ke variables "remember" rakhti hai — even after outer function return ho chuki ho.

def make_counter(start=0):
    count = [start]
    def increment():
        count[0] += 1
        return count[0]
    return increment  # Closure

api_calls = make_counter()
print(api_calls())  # 1
print(api_calls())  # 2
Python

Decorator

Function ko wrap karta hai — bina original function change kiye behavior add karna. Logging, retry, auth mein use.

def log_call(func):
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        print(f"Calling {func.__name__}")
        result = func(*args, **kwargs)
        print(f"Done: {func.__name__}")
        return result
    return wrapper

@log_call
def search_web(query):
    return f"Results for {query}"

search_web("RAG")
Python
⚡ Production Decorator — Retry Logic
import time
def retry(max_attempts=3, delay=1):
    def decorator(func):
        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
            for attempt in range(max_attempts):
                try: return func(*args, **kwargs)
                except Exception as e:
                    if attempt == max_attempts - 1: raise
                    time.sleep(delay * (2**attempt))  # Exponential backoff
        return wrapper
    return decorator

@retry(max_attempts=3, delay=2)
def call_llm_api(prompt): pass  # Real API call yahan aayega
Python
📝 Chapter 1.4 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek function calculate_tokens(text) likho jo approximate token count return kare (words × 1.3).
  2. Ek @timer decorator banao jo function execution time measure kare.
  3. Lambda use karke tool results ko score ke basis pe filter karo (sirf score > 0.8 wale).
  4. Ek recursive function flatten(nested_list) banao jo deeply nested list ko flat kare.
16
Chapter 1.5

Object-Oriented Programming

"Real world ki cheezein classes mein bandho, code organize ho jayega."
OOP ek programming paradigm hai jisme hum code ko objects ke around organize karte hain — real world entities ki tarah. Ek AI agent khud ek object hai jiske paas state (memory, tools) aur behavior (plan, execute, reflect) hai. LangChain, CrewAI jaise sare frameworks OOP pe based hain.

1.5.1 Class aur Object

Diagram 1.6 — Class vs Object
Class (Blueprint)

Template / Design
class Agent:
  name, tools, memory
  plan(), execute(), reflect()

Objects (Instances)

research_bot = Agent("ResearchBot")
code_bot = Agent("CodeBot")
email_bot = Agent("EmailBot")
(Har ek alag state ke saath)

class Agent:
    """AI Agent base class"""

    # Class variable (shared by all instances)
    agent_count = 0

    def __init__(self, name: str, tools: list):
        """Constructor — object create hote waqt chalta hai"""
        self.name = name                 # Instance variable
        self.tools = tools
        self.memory = []
        self.iteration = 0
        Agent.agent_count += 1          # Class variable update

    def think(self, query: str) -> str:
        """LLM se reasoning"""
        self.memory.append({"thought": query})
        return f"[{self.name}] Thinking about: {query}"

    def act(self, tool_name: str, *args) -> str:
        if tool_name not in self.tools:
            return f"Error: Tool {tool_name} not available"
        return f"[{self.name}] Using {tool_name} with {args}"

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        """Object ka string representation"""
        return f"Agent(name={self.name!r}, tools={self.tools})"

# Object creation
bot = Agent("ResearchBot", ["search", "summarize"])
print(bot.think("What is RAG?"))
print(bot.act("search", "RAG papers 2024"))
print(Agent.agent_count)  # 1
Python

1.5.2 Inheritance — Parent se Sikhna

class ResearchAgent(Agent):   # Agent se inherit kar raha hai
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        super().__init__(name, tools=["search", "arxiv", "summarize"])
        self.papers_found = []

    def research(self, topic: str) -> str:
        self.think(f"Researching: {topic}")      # Parent method
        result = self.act("search", topic)        # Parent method
        self.papers_found.append(topic)
        return result

class CodingAgent(Agent):
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        super().__init__(name, tools=["code_interpreter", "debugger"])

    def code(self, task: str) -> str:
        return self.act("code_interpreter", task)

researcher = ResearchAgent("ResearchBot")
print(researcher.research("LLM fine-tuning"))
Python

1.5.3 Key OOP Concepts

ConceptDefinitionAI Agent Example
EncapsulationData aur methods ek saath band karna; private data se protect karnaAgent ki internal state (API key) ko private rakhna
InheritanceParent class ke properties/methods child ko milnaBaseAgent → ResearchAgent → SpecializedAgent
PolymorphismSame method ka alag alag objects mein alag behaviorHar agent ka alag execute() implementation
AbstractionComplex implementation hide karo, simple interface doagent.run(task) — andar kya ho raha nahi pata

1.5.4 Magic Methods (Dunder Methods)

Methods jo double underscore se shuru aur khatam hote hain. Python inhe automatic call karta hai specific situations mein.

MethodKab call hota haiUse
__init__Object create hone peInstance variables set karo
__str__str(obj) ya print(obj)Human-readable string
__repr__Debug/REPL meinDeveloper-facing string
__len__len(obj)Memory/context size
__call__obj(args)Object ko function ki tarah call karo
__enter__/__exit__with statementResource management (DB connection)
📝 Chapter 1.5 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek Tool class banao jisme name, description, aur execute(input) method ho.
  2. Tool se inherit karke SearchTool aur CalculatorTool banao, execute() override karo.
  3. __call__ implement karo taaki tool("query") ki tarah call kar sako.
  4. Ek Memory class banao jo conversation history store kare aur __len__ se message count return kare.
19
Chapter 1.6

Error Handling

"Galat input, network fail, API error — expect the unexpected."
Production AI systems crash nahi karte — wo gracefully handle karte hain. Ek agent 24/7 run kar raha hai — network fail ho, API timeout ho, user galat input de — agent ko har situation handle karni aani chahiye. Error handling isi ke liye hai.

1.6.1 try / except / else / finally

Diagram 1.7 — Exception Handling Flow
try: block
↓
No Error
↙
Error Raised
↘
else: block
Success path
except: block
Handle error
↓               ↓
finally: block
HAMESHA chalta hai
import requests
import logging

def call_llm_api(prompt: str, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
    """Robust LLM API call with proper error handling"""
    try:
        response = requests.post(
            "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
            json={"model": "gpt-4", "messages": [{"role":"user","content":prompt}]},
            timeout=timeout
        )
        response.raise_for_status()       # 4xx/5xx pe exception raise karo
        return response.json()

    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
        logging.error("API timeout after %ds", timeout)
        return {"error": "timeout", "retry": True}

    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
        if e.response.status_code == 429:   # Rate limit
            return {"error": "rate_limit", "retry": True}
        return {"error": str(e), "retry": False}

    except Exception as e:
        logging.exception("Unexpected error in LLM call")
        return {"error": str(e), "retry": False}

    finally:
        logging.info("LLM API call completed")    # Hamesha log karo
Python

1.6.2 Custom Exceptions

class AgentError(Exception):
    """Base exception for all agent errors"""
    pass

class ToolNotFoundError(AgentError):
    def __init__(self, tool_name: str):
        super().__init__(f"Tool '{tool_name}' not registered")
        self.tool_name = tool_name

class ContextLimitError(AgentError):
    def __init__(self, current: int, limit: int):
        super().__init__(f"Context {current} exceeds limit {limit}")
        self.current, self.limit = current, limit

# Usage
try:
    raise ToolNotFoundError("web_scraper")
except ToolNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Handled: {e} — using fallback tool")
Python

1.6.3 Logging — print() Mat Karo Production Mein

import logging

# Production logging setup
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s',
    handlers=[
        logging.StreamHandler(),              # Console
        logging.FileHandler('agent.log')    # File
    ]
)
logger = logging.getLogger('agent.main')

logger.debug("Debug: tool input = %s", tool_input)    # Dev only
logger.info("Agent started iteration %d", iteration)   # Normal ops
logger.warning("Context near limit: %d tokens", tokens) # Watch out
logger.error("Tool failed: %s", error_msg)             # Error
logger.critical("Agent crashed — shutting down")       # Fatal
Python
🧠 Exception Hierarchy — Yaad Rakho
BaseException → Exception → (ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, IndexError, AttributeError, RuntimeError, OSError, ...)
Hamesha specific exception pakdo — except Exception last resort hai, except BaseException kabhi mat karo production mein.
📝 Chapter 1.6 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek function likho jo user se number input le, ValueError handle kare agar number nahi diya.
  2. Custom exception MaxRetriesError banao. Ek retry loop mein 3 attempts ke baad ise raise karo.
  3. Ek JSON file read karo — file missing aur invalid JSON dono errors handle karo with proper logging.
21
Chapter 1.7

File Handling

"Data disk pe hai — use padho, process karo, wapas likho."
AI agents ko data kahin se toh leni hogi — local files, PDFs, CSVs, JSON configs. File handling ek essential skill hai. Context mein bataya gaya har document — agent ke liye ek file hi thi pehle.

1.7.1 Text Files — Read, Write, Append

# Context manager use karo — auto-close guaranteed
with open("knowledge_base.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    content = f.read()         # Poora file
    lines = f.readlines()     # List of lines

with open("output.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("Agent response here\n")

with open("log.txt", "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("New entry appended\n")
Python

1.7.2 JSON — Agent ka Sab Se Zaroori Format

import json

# Read JSON — API responses, configs
with open("config.json", "r") as f:
    config = json.load(f)     # dict mein convert

# Write JSON — agent state save karo
agent_state = {"iteration": 5, "tasks_done": ["search", "summarize"]}
with open("state.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(agent_state, f, indent=2)

# String se/ko convert karna
json_str = json.dumps(agent_state)     # dict → string
parsed = json.loads(json_str)          # string → dict
Python

1.7.3 CSV — Tabular Data

import csv

# Read CSV — dataset processing
with open("results.csv", "r") as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    for row in reader:
        print(row["query"], row["score"])

# Write CSV — evaluation results save karo
with open("eval.csv", "w", newline="") as f:
    writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=["query", "response", "score"])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerow({"query": "What is RAG?", "response": "...", "score": 0.9})
Python

1.7.4 pathlib — Modern File Paths

from pathlib import Path

data_dir = Path("./data")
data_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)     # Directory banao

# All JSON files list karo
for file in data_dir.glob("*.json"):
    print(file.name, file.stat().st_size)

config_path = Path("config") / "agent.json"  # OS-safe path joining
Python
📝 Chapter 1.7 — Mini Exercises
  1. Agent conversation history ko JSON file mein save karo aur wapas load karo.
  2. Ek directory ke sare .txt files count karo aur unka total size print karo.
  3. CSV file padho, scores ko filter karo (> 0.8), aur result nayi CSV mein save karo.
23
Chapter 1.8

Data Structures & Algorithms

"Sahi data structure = fast code. Galat data structure = production mein rona."
Data structures batate hain data ko organize kaise karo. Algorithms batate hain problem solve kaise karo. AI agent mein retrieval speed, memory lookup, task queue — sab DSA pe depend karta hai. Interview mein bhi yahi puchha jata hai!

1.8.1 Core Data Structures

Array / List — O(1) access

Index pe direct access. Insert/delete at end: O(1). Middle mein: O(n). AI use: token sequences, retrieved chunks.

Stack (LIFO) — O(1) push/pop

Last In, First Out. Function call stack, undo operations, DFS traversal. Python mein list use karo.

Queue (FIFO) — O(1) enqueue/dequeue

First In, First Out. Task queues, BFS, message queues. collections.deque use karo.

HashMap / Dict — O(1) avg lookup

Key-value pairs. Tool registry, cache, memoization. Python dict hash-table backed hai.

Tree — O(log n) operations

Hierarchical data. File systems, decision trees, task decomposition trees.

Graph — O(V+E) traversal

Nodes aur edges. Knowledge graphs, multi-agent communication, LangGraph.

from collections import deque, defaultdict

# Stack — agent reasoning trace
reasoning_stack = []
reasoning_stack.append("Understand query")
reasoning_stack.append("Search for context")
reasoning_stack.append("Generate response")
last_step = reasoning_stack.pop()   # "Generate response"

# Queue — task queue for agent
task_queue = deque(["task1", "task2", "task3"])
task_queue.appendleft("urgent_task")  # High priority task
current_task = task_queue.popleft()  # "urgent_task"

# HashMap — tool registry
tool_registry = {}
tool_registry["search"] = lambda q: f"Search results for {q}"
tool_registry["calc"] = lambda expr: eval(expr)

# Memoization — expensive computations cache karo
embedding_cache = {}
def get_embedding(text: str) -> list:
    if text not in embedding_cache:
        embedding_cache[text] = [0.1, 0.2, 0.3]   # API call yahan
    return embedding_cache[text]
Python

1.8.2 Big O Notation — Performance Socho

NotationNameExample10 items → 1000 items
O(1)ConstantDict lookupSame speed
O(log n)LogarithmicBinary search10x → 30x slower
O(n)LinearLinear search100x slower
O(n log n)LinearithmicMerge sort~330x slower
O(n²)QuadraticBubble sort10,000x slower

1.8.3 Searching

# Linear Search — O(n)
def linear_search(arr, target):
    for i, val in enumerate(arr):
        if val == target:
            return i
    return -1

# Binary Search — O(log n) — sorted array pe
def binary_search(arr, target):
    left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
    while left <= right:
        mid = (left + right) // 2
        if arr[mid] == target: return mid
        elif arr[mid] < target: left = mid + 1
        else: right = mid - 1
    return -1
Python
⚡ RAG mein DSA Connection
Vector similarity search (cosine similarity) essentially nearest-neighbor search hai. FAISS, Pinecone internally special index structures (HNSW, IVF) use karte hain jo O(log n) pe kaam karte hain — O(n) brute force se zyada fast.
📝 Chapter 1.8 — Mini Exercises
  1. deque use karke ek task queue banao: tasks add karo, execute karo (popleft), aur done tasks track karo.
  2. Memoization decorator @memoize banao jo kisi bhi function ke results cache kare.
  3. Binary search implement karo aur test karo — 1000 elements mein kitne steps lage vs linear search.
  4. Ek graph (dict of lists) represent karo jo agents ke beech communication channels dikhaye. BFS se traversal karo.
25
Chapter 1.9

APIs & Networking

"AI agent sirf apne andar nahi rehta — woh duniya se baat karta hai APIs ke zariye."
AI agents external services se baat karte hain — LLM APIs, search APIs, database APIs, tool APIs. Networking ka basic samajhna zaroori hai taaki errors debug ho sakein, timeouts handle ho sakein, authentication set ho sake.

1.9.1 HTTP Basics

Diagram 1.8 — HTTP Request/Response Cycle
AI Agent
(Client)
→ HTTP Request →
Server
(OpenAI/Anthropic)
Request: Method + URL + Headers + Body
Agent processes
response
← HTTP Response ←
Status + Body
(JSON)
HTTP MethodUseAI Agent Example
GETData retrieve karoTool results fetch karo, model list lo
POSTData bhejo / create karoLLM completion request, embedding generate
PUTData update karo (replace)Agent config update karo
PATCHPartial updateSpecific field update
DELETEDelete karoConversation history delete

1.9.2 Status Codes — Jo Samajhne Chahiye

CodeMeaningAgent Action
200 OKSuccessResponse process karo
201 CreatedResource createdNew ID save karo
400 Bad RequestGalat inputRequest fix karo, retry
401 UnauthorizedAuth failAPI key check karo
403 ForbiddenPermission nahiPermissions verify karo
404 Not FoundResource nahi milaURL check karo
429 Too Many RequestsRate limitBackoff lagao, wait karo
500 Server ErrorServer crashRetry with backoff

1.9.3 REST API Call — Python mein

import requests
import os

def chat_completion(messages: list, model: str = "gpt-4o") -> str:
    """OpenAI Chat Completion API call"""
    api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")   # Key env mein rakho!
    if not api_key:
        raise ValueError("OPENAI_API_KEY not set")

    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }
    payload = {
        "model": model,
        "messages": messages,
        "temperature": 0.7,
        "max_tokens": 1024
    }
    response = requests.post(
        "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions",
        headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=60
    )
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]

# Usage
reply = chat_completion([{"role": "user", "content": "What is RAG?"}])
Python

1.9.4 Authentication Methods

MethodHowUse Case
API KeyHeader: Authorization: Bearer keyOpenAI, Anthropic, most LLM APIs
OAuth 2.0Access token via flowGoogle, GitHub, enterprise apps
JWTSigned token in headerYour own backend APIs
Basic AuthBase64(user:pass) in headerLegacy systems (avoid in prod)
⚠️ Security — API Keys Kabhi Hardcode Mat Karo!
api_key = "sk-abc..." — YEH GALAT HAI. Hamesha environment variables use karo: os.getenv("API_KEY"). Production mein secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault) use karo.
📝 Chapter 1.9 — Mini Exercises
  1. Free API (e.g., https://api.agify.io/?name=Alice) pe GET request karo aur response print karo.
  2. Status code 429 ke liye exponential backoff retry function likho (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s delays).
  3. Ek APIClient class banao jisme get(), post() methods hon jo headers automatically add kare aur errors handle kare.
29
Chapter 1.10

Databases

"Memory RAM mein hoti hai, data disk pe — database dono ke beech bridge hai."
AI agents ko persistent data chahiye — user preferences, conversation history, knowledge base, evaluation results. SQL databases structured data ke liye hain, NoSQL flexible/unstructured data ke liye. Vector databases embeddings ke liye hain (Part 7 mein detail se).

1.10.1 SQL Basics — Structured Query Language

Diagram 1.9 — Relational Database Concept
Table: users
id | name | email
1 | Alice | ...
2 | Bob | ...
Table: conversations
id | user_id | text
1 | 1 | Hello...
2 | 1 | Tell me...
Table: memories
id | user_id | fact
1 | 1 | Likes Python
2 | 1 | Engineer

Tables aapas mein foreign keys se judi hain. SQL se join karke related data ek saath lao.

-- CRUD Operations — Create, Read, Update, Delete

-- Create table
CREATE TABLE agent_memories (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    fact TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Insert (Create)
INSERT INTO agent_memories (user_id, fact) VALUES (1, 'User prefers Python');

-- Select (Read)
SELECT * FROM agent_memories WHERE user_id = 1 ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Update
UPDATE agent_memories SET fact = 'User prefers Python 3.11' WHERE id = 1;

-- Delete
DELETE FROM agent_memories WHERE id = 1;

-- JOIN — user info ke saath memories fetch karo
SELECT u.name, m.fact
FROM users u
JOIN agent_memories m ON u.id = m.user_id
WHERE u.id = 1;
SQL

1.10.2 Python se SQLite

import sqlite3
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def get_db(path="agent.db"):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(path)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row   # dict-like rows
    try:
        yield conn
        conn.commit()
    except Exception:
        conn.rollback()
        raise
    finally:
        conn.close()

# Usage
with get_db() as db:
    db.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memories (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, fact TEXT)")
    db.execute("INSERT INTO memories (fact) VALUES (?)", ("User likes Python",))

with get_db() as db:
    rows = db.execute("SELECT * FROM memories").fetchall()
    for row in rows:
        print(dict(row))
Python

1.10.3 SQL vs NoSQL

FeatureSQL (PostgreSQL, SQLite)NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis)
Data structureTables with fixed schemaDocuments, key-value, graphs
SchemaRigid — define pehle karoFlexible — kuch bhi store karo
ACID transactionsFull supportLimited (Redis: atomic ops)
Best forUser data, financial recordsConversation history, cache, logs
AI Agent useUser profiles, eval resultsSession state, fast memory
📝 Chapter 1.10 — Mini Exercises
  1. SQLite mein ek conversations table banao. Messages insert karo, phir ek user ke sare messages fetch karo.
  2. SQL JOIN likhao jo conversations ko user name ke saath dikhaye.
  3. Ek ConversationDB class banao jo save_message(), get_history(user_id), clear_history() methods provide kare.
32
Chapter 1.11

Async Programming

"Wait mat karo — dusra kaam shuru karo. Yahi async ka funda hai."
AI agents bahut API calls karte hain — LLM API, search API, tool APIs. Synchronous code ek request ka wait karta hai phir agle ko — slow. Async code multiple requests concurrently bhejta hai — fast. Production agents ko async aana hi chahiye.

1.11.1 Sync vs Async

Diagram 1.10 — Synchronous vs Asynchronous Execution
Synchronous (Slow)
▶ API Call 1 (2s wait)
▶ API Call 2 (2s wait)
▶ API Call 3 (2s wait)
Total: 6 seconds
Asynchronous (Fast)
▶ API Call 1 (start) ──┐
▶ API Call 2 (start) ──┤ Concurrent
▶ API Call 3 (start) ──┘
Total: ~2 seconds ✅ 
import asyncio
import aiohttp

# Async function — 'async def' se define hoti hai
async def fetch_tool_result(session: aiohttp.ClientSession, tool_url: str) -> dict:
    """Single tool API call"""
    async with session.get(tool_url) as response:
        return await response.json()   # await se wait karo

async def run_parallel_tools(tool_urls: list) -> list:
    """Multiple tools concurrently chalao"""
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        tasks = [fetch_tool_result(session, url) for url in tool_urls]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)  # Sab ek saath
        return results

# Synchronous context se async function call karna
urls = ["https://api.tool1.com", "https://api.tool2.com"]
results = asyncio.run(run_parallel_tools(urls))
Python

1.11.2 asyncio Key Concepts

ConceptDefinitionCode
CoroutineAsync function jo yield kar sakti haiasync def func()
awaitCoroutine complete hone ka wait karoresult = await coro()
TaskBackground mein run hone wali coroutineasyncio.create_task()
gather()Multiple coroutines concurrently run karoawait asyncio.gather(*coros)
Event LoopAsync tasks ko manage karne wala engineasyncio.run(main())

1.11.3 Threads vs Processes vs Async

ModelBest ForPython hai?
Async/AwaitI/O bound: API calls, file ops, DB✅ asyncio
ThreadsI/O bound, legacy code integration✅ threading — GIL limitation
ProcessesCPU bound: ML inference, image processing✅ multiprocessing — true parallel
⚡ Agent Framework mein Async
LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI sab async support karte hain. FastAPI automatically async handle karta hai. asyncio.gather() se multiple LLM calls parallel karo — latency 3x-5x reduce hoti hai.
📝 Chapter 1.11 — Mini Exercises
  1. asyncio.sleep() use karke 3 "API calls" (simulated) ko concurrent run karo. Total time measure karo.
  2. asyncio.gather() vs sequential await ka time compare karo.
  3. Ek async function with_timeout(coro, seconds) banao jo asyncio.wait_for use kare.
35
Chapter 1.12

Development Tools

"Sahi tools se kaam double fast hota hai — ek din setup mein lagao, lifetime bacchao."
Ek professional AI engineer sirf code nahi likhta — wo tools use karta hai jo code ko manage, test, containerize, aur deploy karte hain. Yeh tools industry standard hain — inke bina production pe jaana mushkil hai.

1.12.1 Git & GitHub — Version Control

Diagram 1.11 — Git Workflow
Working
Directory
git add →
Staging
Area
git commit →
Local
Repo
git push →
GitHub
(Remote)
# Essential Git commands — AI project workflow
git init                            # Naya project initialize
git clone https://github.com/...   # Existing project download

git add .                           # Sab files stage karo
git commit -m "feat: add RAG pipeline"  # Commit
git push origin main               # GitHub pe upload

git checkout -b feature/agent-memory   # Naya branch banao
git merge feature/agent-memory         # Branch merge karo

# .gitignore — sensitive files commit mat karo!
echo ".env" >> .gitignore
echo "__pycache__/" >> .gitignore
echo "*.pyc" >> .gitignore
Bash

1.12.2 Virtual Environment — Dependencies Isolate Karo

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv .venv

# Activate (Windows)
.venv\Scripts\activate

# Activate (Mac/Linux)
source .venv/bin/activate

# Install packages
pip install langchain openai chromadb python-dotenv

# Requirements file — reproducibility ke liye
pip freeze > requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements.txt    # Kisi aur machine pe install
Bash

1.12.3 Docker — "Mere machine pe chalta tha" wali problem solve

📖 Definition
Docker containers banata hai — lightweight, isolated environments jisme application aur uski saari dependencies pack hoti hain. Kisi bhi machine pe same way mein run karta hai. 
# Dockerfile — AI Agent ke liye
FROM python:3.11-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Dependencies pehle copy karo (caching ke liye)
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Code copy karo
COPY . .

# Environment variables
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# App start command
CMD ["python", "agent.py"]
Dockerfile
# Docker commands
docker build -t my-agent .          # Image build karo
docker run -e OPENAI_API_KEY=$KEY my-agent  # Run karo
docker ps                           # Running containers dekho
docker logs container_id            # Logs dekho
Bash

1.12.4 .env Files — Secrets Ka Sahi Jagah

# .env file (KABHI GITHUB PE PUSH MAT KARO!)
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-abc123...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=ant-xyz...
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost/db
DEBUG=True
.env
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os

load_dotenv()  # .env file load karo
api_key = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///default.db")
Python
ToolPurposeInstall / Use
VS CodeCode editor — Python, AI pluginscode.visualstudio.com
Jupyter NotebookInteractive Python — experimentspip install jupyter
PostmanAPI testing — LLM APIs test karopostman.com
GitVersion controlsudo apt install git
DockerContainerizationdocker.com
pyenvMultiple Python versionscurl pyenv.run | bash
📝 Chapter 1.12 — Mini Exercises
  1. Ek naya Python project setup karo: virtual env, .env file, .gitignore, aur requirements.txt.
  2. Git se ek feature branch banao, 3 commits karo, aur main mein merge karo. Log dekho.
  3. Ek simple "Hello Agent" Python script ke liye Dockerfile likho. Build aur run karo.
38
Part 1 — Summary

Programming Foundations

Poora part ek baar mein revise karo
🧠 The Big Picture

Programming ek language hai jisme hum machines se baat karte hain. Data types values ko represent karte hain. Control flow decisions aur loops manage karta hai. Functions code ko reusable banate hain. OOP real-world concepts ko model karta hai. Error handling production ko reliable banata hai. File/DB/API handling data aur external systems se connect karta hai. Async speed deta hai. Dev tools sab kuch organized rakhte hain.

Chapter-wise Key Takeaways

ChapterCore ConceptAI Agent Relevance
1.1 Programming BasicsVariables, operators, I/O, type conversion, f-stringsPrompt building, config management
1.2 Data Typesint, float, bool, str, list, dict, set, tuple, NoneMessages list, agent state dict, tool set
1.3 Control Flowif/elif/else, for, while, break, generatorsAgent loop, tool selection, chunk processing
1.4 FunctionsParameters, lambdas, recursion, closures, decoratorsRetry decorator, tool functions, task decomposition
1.5 OOPClasses, inheritance, polymorphism, magic methodsAgent class, Tool class, Memory class
1.6 Error Handlingtry/except, custom exceptions, loggingAPI failures, timeout handling, graceful degradation
1.7 File HandlingText, JSON, CSV, pathlibKnowledge base, config files, eval results
1.8 DSAList, Stack, Queue, HashMap, Big OTask queues, embedding cache, tool registry
1.9 APIs & NetworkingHTTP, REST, status codes, authLLM API calls, tool integrations
1.10 DatabasesSQL CRUD, joins, Python-SQLite, NoSQLConversation history, user memory, eval store
1.11 Asyncasync/await, asyncio.gather, event loopParallel tool calls, fast LLM responses
1.12 Dev ToolsGit, venv, Docker, .env, VS CodeProduction deployment, team collaboration

🏗️ Mini Project — Simple AI Agent Shell

Yeh project Part 1 ke sab concepts ek saath use karta hai:

import asyncio, json, os, logging
from pathlib import Path
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

class SimpleAgent:
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        self.name = name
        self.history: list = []
        self.tools: dict = {}

    def register_tool(self, name: str, func):
        self.tools[name] = func

    async def run(self, query: str) -> str:
        logging.info("[%s] Processing: %s", self.name, query)
        self.history.append({"role": "user", "content": query})
        try:
            # Tool selection (simple keyword matching)
            tool = "echo"  # Real: LLM decides
            if tool in self.tools:
                result = self.tools[tool](query)
            else:
                result = f"Processed: {query}"
            self.history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": result})
            return result
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error("Agent error: %s", e)
            return f"Error: {e}"

    def save_history(self, path: str = "history.json"):
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2)

async def main():
    agent = SimpleAgent("MyFirstAgent")
    agent.register_tool("echo", lambda q: f"Echo: {q}")
    result = await agent.run("Hello, Agent!")
    print(result)
    agent.save_history()

asyncio.run(main())
Python — Part 1 Mini Project
🧠 Part 1 Interview Checklist
  • Mutable vs Immutable types examples de sako
  • List, Dict, Set ka time complexity yaad ho (lookup, insert, delete)
  • Decorator aur closure explain kar sako with code
  • async/await ko simple words mein explain kar sako
  • Custom exception hierarchy bana sako
  • API call ke sab common error codes handle kar sako
  • Docker aur .env ka purpose explain kar sako

Part 1 Complete! 🎉

40

for Artificial Intelligence

📐 Linear Algebra 🎲 Probability & Statistics ∂ Calculus 🐍 Python Code 🧠 Hinglish
