Chapter 1 · NLP Foundations

🗣️ Natural Language Processing ke Fundamentals

Text ko machine-readable banane ki journey — raw words se meaning tak

Jab bhi hum LLMs ke baare mein baat karte hain, toh ek sawaal aata hai: Machine text ko kaise samajhti hai? Humans ke liye language natural hai, lekin computer ke liye toh sab kuch numbers hota hai! Yahan NLP (Natural Language Processing) ka role aata hai.

📌NLP Kya Hai?

Natural Language Processing (NLP) AI ki woh branch hai jo computers ko human language — text ya speech — ko samajhne, process karne, aur generate karne ki ability deti hai. Jaise Google Translate, ChatGPT, Siri — sab NLP use karte hain.

NLP Pipeline — Raw Text se Meaning Tak
1.1 Tokenization

🔤 Tokenization — Text ko Todna

Kya hai Tokenization?

Tokenization ek aisi process hai jisme hum ek bade text string ko chhote meaningful pieces (tokens) mein todte hain. Ye tokens words, subwords, characters, ya phir punctuation marks ho sakte hain.

💡Beginner Intuition

Soch — jaise hum ek sentence ko bol rahe hain "Main school jaata hoon" — toh yeh sentence ke 4 words hain. Ye 4 words hi basically tokens hain! Machine bhi text ko aise hi chhote pieces mein todti hai process karne ke liye.

Tokenization ke Types

TypeKya Karta HaiExampleUse Case
Word TokenizationText ko words par split karta hai"Hello World" → ["Hello", "World"]Basic NLP tasks
Sentence TokenizationText ko sentences mein todta haiPara → ["Sent1.", "Sent2."]Summarization
Subword TokenizationRare words ko subwords mein todta hai"playing" → ["play", "##ing"]BERT, GPT models
Character TokenizationHar character alag token"Hi" → ["H","i"]Language modeling
BPE (Byte Pair Encoding)Common pairs ko merge karta haiGPT-style tokenizationLLMs

BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) — GPT ka Secret

Modern LLMs jaise GPT, Claude, Llama sab BPE tokenization use karte hain. Iska idea simple hai:

1
Start with characters

Pehle har character alag token hota hai: "l", "o", "w" etc.

2
Find common pairs

Sabse zyada baar aane wali character pairs dhundho: "l"+"o" = "lo"

3
Merge them

Pair ko merge karo aur vocabulary mein add karo.

4
Repeat

Desired vocabulary size tak repeat karo (e.g., 50,000 tokens).

Token Visualization — "Unbelievable" ko kaise tokenize karte hain
Un believ able → 3 tokens
I love eating biryani ! → 5 tokens
Python — Tokenization Example 
# Basic Word Tokenization using NLTK
import nltk
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize, sent_tokenize

text = "Main ek AI engineer hoon. Mujhe Python bahut pasand hai!"

# Word tokenization
words = word_tokenize(text)
print("Words:", words)
# Output: ['Main', 'ek', 'AI', 'engineer', 'hoon', '.', 'Mujhe', 'Python', ...]

# Sentence tokenization
sents = sent_tokenize(text)
print("Sentences:", sents)
# Output: ['Main ek AI engineer hoon.', 'Mujhe Python bahut pasand hai!']

# ---- GPT-style BPE Tokenization (tiktoken) ----
import tiktoken

enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model("gpt-4")
text2 = "Artificial Intelligence is amazing!"

tokens = enc.encode(text2)
print("Token IDs:", tokens)
# Output: [9470, 35915, 11, 1002, 374, 8056, 0]

print("Total tokens:", len(tokens))
# Output: 7

# Token wapas text mein convert karo
for token in tokens:
    print(repr(enc.decode([token])), "→ ID:", token)
⚠️Common Mistake

Bahut log sochte hain ki 1 word = 1 token. Yeh GALAT hai! "unbelievable" = 3 tokens ho sakta hai. Iska directly asar padta hai LLM ke cost aur context window par. Always token count check karo, word count nahi!

1.2 Stemming

✂️ Stemming — Root Word Dhundho

Stemming ek aise process hai jisme hum kisi word ke ending (suffix) ko hataate hain taaki us word ka root (stem) mile. Yeh process thodi rough hoti hai — exact root nahi, sirf approximate stem milta hai.

✅ Stemming kab kaam aata hai
  • Search engines mein (fast retrieval)
  • Jab speed important ho accuracy se zyada
  • Information retrieval tasks
  • Simple text classification
❌ Stemming ki limitations
  • "studies" → "studi" (galat English word)
  • "better" → "better" (no change, missed)
  • Context ko samajhta nahi
  • Over-stemming (words galat ban jaate hain)
Python — Stemming 
from nltk.stem import PorterStemmer, SnowballStemmer

porter = PorterStemmer()
snowball = SnowballStemmer("english")

words = ["running", "studies", "happily", "better", "eating"]

print("Word         Porter      Snowball")
print("-"*40)
for w in words:
    print(f"{w:13} {porter.stem(w):12} {snowball.stem(w)}")

# Output:
# Word         Porter      Snowball
# ----------------------------------------
# running      run         run
# studies      studi       studi    ← Thoda rough!
# happily      happili     happili
# better       better      better   ← Kuch nahi hua
# eating       eat         eat
1.3 Lemmatization

📚 Lemmatization — Sahi Root Word

Lemmatization bhi stemming ki tarah root form dhundti hai, lekin yeh grammatically correct root word return karti hai — jo dictionary mein milta hai (lemma). Yeh stemming se zyada smart hai kyunki yeh word ka context (POS) bhi consider karti hai.

WordStemming ResultLemmatization ResultSahi?
studiesstudi ❌study ✅Lemma wins
runningrun ✅run ✅Dono sahi
betterbetter ❌good ✅Lemma wins
ateate ❌eat ✅Lemma wins
caringcar ❌❌care ✅Lemma sirf winner
Python — Lemmatization with SpaCy 
import spacy

# SpaCy ka English model load karo
nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")

text = "The children are running and eating their studies better"
doc = nlp(text)

print("Word       → Lemma")
print("-"*25)
for token in doc:
    print(f"{token.text:10} → {token.lemma_}")

# Output:
# Word       → Lemma
# -------------------------
# The        → the
# children   → child
# are        → be
# running    → run
# eating     → eat
# studies    → study
# better     → well
1.4 POS Tagging

🏷️ POS Tagging — Parts of Speech

POS (Part-of-Speech) Tagging ek aisi technique hai jisme hum har word ko uski grammatical category assign karte hain — jaise Noun, Verb, Adjective, Adverb, etc. Yeh NLP ki fundamental step hai.

POS TagMeaningExample
NNNoun (singular)dog, computer, India
NNSNoun (plural)dogs, computers
VBVerb (base form)run, eat, code
VBGVerb (gerund)running, eating
JJAdjectivebeautiful, fast, smart
RBAdverbquickly, happily
PRPPersonal pronounI, he, she, they
INPrepositionin, on, at, for
DTDeterminerthe, a, an
Python — POS Tagging 
import spacy

nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
doc = nlp("The smart AI engineer coded a brilliant solution.")

print(f"{'Token':12} {'POS':8} {'Tag':6} Description")
print("-"*55)
for token in doc:
    print(f"{token.text:12} {token.pos_:8} {token.tag_:6} {spacy.explain(token.tag_)}")

# Output:
# Token        POS      Tag    Description
# -------------------------------------------------------
# The          DET      DT     determiner
# smart        ADJ      JJ     adjective, nominal
# AI           PROPN    NNP    noun, proper, singular
# engineer     NOUN     NN     noun, singular or mass
# coded        VERB     VBD    verb, past tense
# a            DET      DT     determiner
# brilliant    ADJ      JJ     adjective
# solution     NOUN     NN     noun, singular
1.5 Named Entity Recognition (NER)

🔍 NER — Important Names Pakdo

Named Entity Recognition (NER) ek aisi technique hai jo text mein se important real-world entities identify karti hai — jaise persons ke naam, organizations, locations, dates, amounts etc.

👤
PERSON

Elon Musk, Narendra Modi, Sundar Pichai

🏢
ORG

Google, OpenAI, ISRO, Microsoft

📍
GPE

India, Mumbai, Silicon Valley

📅
DATE

2024, Monday, March 15th

💰
MONEY

₹50 crore, $100 million

📊
PERCENT

85%, nearly 30%

Python — Named Entity Recognition 
import spacy

nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")

text = """
    Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla, announced on Monday that 
    the company invested $3 billion in India in 2024.
    The factory will be built in Maharashtra.
"""

doc = nlp(text)

print("Entities Found:")
print("-"*45)
for ent in doc.ents:
    print(f"{ent.text:20} → {ent.label_:10} ({spacy.explain(ent.label_)})")

# Output:
# Entities Found:
# ---------------------------------------------
# Elon Musk            → PERSON     (People, including fictional)
# Tesla                → ORG        (Companies, agencies, institutions)
# Monday               → DATE       (Absolute or relative dates or periods)
# $3 billion           → MONEY      (Monetary values, including unit)
# India                → GPE        (Countries, cities, states)
# 2024                 → DATE       (Absolute or relative dates or periods)
# Maharashtra          → GPE        (Countries, cities, states)
🎯Interview Questions — NLP Foundations
  • Stemming aur Lemmatization mein kya difference hai? Kab kaunsa use karein?
  • BPE (Byte Pair Encoding) tokenization kaise kaam karta hai? GPT mein kyun use hota hai?
  • NER ke real-world applications kya hain? Financial news mein kaise use hoga?
  • POS tagging ka use LLM training mein kya hai?
  • Ek 100-word paragraph mein approximately kitne tokens honge GPT-4 mein?
📝 Chapter 1 Summary — NLP Foundations
  • Tokenization: Text ko tokens (words/subwords) mein todna — BPE modern LLMs ka standard hai
  • Stemming: Fast lekin rough — root word approximately milta hai (Porter, Snowball)
  • Lemmatization: Slow but accurate — dictionary-correct lemma milta hai (SpaCy use karo)
  • POS Tagging: Har word ka grammatical role identify karna — Noun, Verb, Adj etc.
  • NER: Text mein real-world entities (Person, Org, Location) detect karna
AI Agent Engineering Masterbook — Part 5 | Chapter 1 NLP Foundations
Chapter 2 · Transformers

⚡ Transformer Architecture — AI ka Dil

"Attention Is All You Need" — 2017 ka woh paper jisne AI ki duniya badal di

2017 mein Google ke researchers ne ek paper publish kiya: "Attention Is All You Need". Isme ek naya architecture propose kiya gaya — Transformer. Aaj duniya ke sabse powerful AI models (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini) sab Transformer architecture par based hain.

📌Transformer Kyun Important Hai?

Pehle NLP mein RNN (Recurrent Neural Networks) use hote the, jo text ko sequentially (ek-ek word) process karte the. Transformer ne yeh sab badal diya — ab parallel processing possible ho gayi jisme ek baar mein pura text process ho sakta hai. Isi liye training bahut fast ho gayi!

Transformer Architecture Overview
2.1 Attention Mechanism

👁️ Attention Mechanism — Dhyan Kaha Lagayen?

Attention mechanism ka core idea yeh hai: Jab hum ek word ko process karte hain, toh hume sentence ke baaqi words ka kitna importance dena chahiye?

💡Real-World Analogy — Exam Mein Padhna

Socho tum ek question padh rahe ho: "The bank near the river was closed." Yahan "bank" ka matlab hai nadi ka kinara, na ki paisa wali bank. Tumhara brain automatically "river" word ko zyada attention deta hai "bank" ke context ko samajhne ke liye. Yahi kaam Attention Mechanism karta hai!

Attention Ka Mathematical Formula

Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax( Q·Kᵀ / √dₖ ) · V
Jahan Q = Query, K = Key, V = Value, dₖ = Key dimension

Q, K, V Kya Hain? (Intuition)

ComponentAnalogyRole
Query (Q)Librarian se question: "Mujhe AI books chahiye"Current word kya dhundh raha hai
Key (K)Har book ka label: "AI", "Science", "History"Dusre words ki identity/tag
Value (V)Book ka actual contentJo information actually pass hogi
1
Q, K, V matrices compute karo

Input embedding ko 3 alag weight matrices se multiply karo → Q, K, V matrices milti hain.

2
Attention scores compute karo

Q aur K ka dot product lo → similarity scores milte hain. √dₖ se divide karo (scale).

3
Softmax apply karo

Scores ko probabilities mein convert karo (0 to 1) — ye attention weights hain.

4
Value se multiply karo

Attention weights ko V se multiply karo → final context-aware representation milti hai.

2.2 Self-Attention

🔄 Self-Attention — Apne Aap Se Baat

Self-attention mein ek sentence ke words apne hi sentence ke dusre words par attention dete hain. Yahan Q, K, V — teeno ek hi sequence se aate hain (isliye "self").

Self-Attention Visualization — "I love eating biryani"
2.3 Multi-Head Attention

🎭 Multi-Head Attention — Ek Saath Kaafi Nazariye

Ek single attention head ek hi type ka relationship pakad sakta hai. Lekin language mein bahut tarah ke relationships hote hain — grammatical, semantic, coreference etc. Multi-Head Attention ek baar mein multiple attention heads parallel mein chalata hai.

💡Analogy — Multiple Experts

Socho ek doctor, ek lawyer, aur ek engineer ek hi problem dekh rahe hain — teeno alag-alag angle se analyze karte hain. Multi-Head Attention bhi aisa karta hai — har head ek alag "perspective" se text ko analyze karta hai, phir sab combine hote hain!

Input
→
Head 1
Syntax
Head 2
Semantics
Head 3
Coreference
Head 4
Position
... (8 or 16 heads)
Concatenate All Heads
→
Linear Projection
→
Output
Python — Multi-Head Attention (PyTorch) 
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class MultiHeadAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model=512, num_heads=8):
        super().__init__()
        self.d_model = d_model
        self.num_heads = num_heads
        self.d_k = d_model // num_heads  # 512/8 = 64
        
        # Query, Key, Value weight matrices
        self.W_q = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_k = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_v = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
        self.W_o = nn.Linear(d_model, d_model)
    
    def scaled_dot_product_attention(self, Q, K, V):
        # Attention Score = softmax(Q·Kᵀ / √dₖ) · V
        scores = torch.matmul(Q, K.transpose(-2, -1)) / (self.d_k ** 0.5)
        weights = torch.softmax(scores, dim=-1)
        return torch.matmul(weights, V)
    
    def forward(self, x):
        batch_size = x.size(0)
        # Project aur heads mein split karo
        Q = self.W_q(x).view(batch_size, -1, self.num_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        K = self.W_k(x).view(batch_size, -1, self.num_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        V = self.W_v(x).view(batch_size, -1, self.num_heads, self.d_k).transpose(1,2)
        
        attn = self.scaled_dot_product_attention(Q, K, V)
        attn = attn.transpose(1,2).contiguous().view(batch_size, -1, self.d_model)
        return self.W_o(attn)

# Usage
mha = MultiHeadAttention(d_model=512, num_heads=8)
x = torch.randn(2, 10, 512)  # batch=2, seq_len=10, d_model=512
output = mha(x)
print("Output shape:", output.shape)  # torch.Size([2, 10, 512])
2.4 Encoder & Decoder

🏗️ Encoder aur Decoder — Yin aur Yang

FeatureEncoderDecoder
KaamInput ko samjhna (understanding)Output generate karna (generation)
Attention TypeBidirectional Self-AttentionMasked Self-Attention + Cross-Attention
ContextPoora sequence ek saath dekh sakta haiSirf past tokens dekh sakta hai
ModelsBERT, RoBERTaGPT family
Use CasesClassification, NER, Q&AText generation, Chat
Encoder+DecoderT5, BART — Translation, Summarization
2.5 Positional Encoding

📍 Positional Encoding — Order Batao!

Transformer mein words parallel process hote hain — isliye usse nahi pata ki kaun sa word pehle hai aur kaun baad mein! Positional Encoding is problem ko solve karta hai. Har position ko ek unique mathematical signal assign kiya jaata hai.

PE(pos, 2i) = sin(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))
PE(pos, 2i+1) = cos(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))
Jahan pos = position, i = dimension index, d_model = model dimension
💡Analogy — GPS Coordinates

Positional encoding bilkul GPS coordinates ki tarah hai. Jaise GPS bata deta hai ki "Main Mumbai ke is specific point par hoon", waise Positional Encoding bata deta hai ki "Main sentence ka 3rd word hoon." Model in coordinates ko word embeddings mein add karke position information encode karta hai.

🎯Interview Questions — Transformers
  • "Attention Is All You Need" paper 2017 mein kya propose kiya? RNN se kaise better hai?
  • Q, K, V matrices ka intuitive explanation do. √dₖ se kyun divide karte hain?
  • Multi-head attention single head se better kyun hai?
  • Encoder-only vs Decoder-only models mein kya difference hai? Examples do.
  • Masked self-attention kya hai aur GPT mein kyun zaroori hai?
  • Positional encoding ke bina kya problem hogi?
📝 Chapter 2 Summary — Transformers
  • Transformer: 2017 ka revolutionary architecture — parallel processing, no RNN
  • Attention: Q·Kᵀ/√dₖ softmax × V — context-aware word representations
  • Self-Attention: Same sequence ke words apas mein attend karte hain
  • Multi-Head: 8-16 parallel attention heads — different types of relationships
  • Encoder: Bidirectional understanding (BERT) | Decoder: Causal generation (GPT)
  • Positional Encoding: Sin/Cos signals jo word order batate hain
AI Agent Engineering Masterbook — Part 5 | Chapter 2 Transformer Architecture
Chapter 3 · LLM Concepts

🧠 Large Language Model Concepts

Tokens se lekar Temperature tak — LLM ke har ek gear ko samjho

LLMs samajhne ke liye kuch fundamental concepts hain jo har AI engineer ko pata hone chahiye. Ye concepts directly affect karte hain aapke model ki quality, cost, speed aur reliability.

3.1 Tokens & Tokenization in LLMs

🎫 Tokens — LLM ki Currency

LLMs ke liye token wo basic unit hai jis par sab kuch depend karta hai — pricing, speed, context window, sab kuch! Ek rough rule hai: 1 token ≈ 0.75 words (English mein).

TextApproximate TokensNote
"Hello"1 tokenCommon short words = 1 token
"unbelievable"3 tokensLong/uncommon words = multiple tokens
1 paragraph (100 words)~130 tokensEnglish text average
1 page (500 words)~650 tokensStandard document page
1 book (300 pages)~200,000 tokensApproximately
Hindi/Urdu text3-4x more tokensNon-English costs more!
⚠️Hindi/Hinglish Tokenization Warning

Hindi ya Devanagari text mein zyada tokens lagte hain! Ek Hindi word ke liye 3-5 tokens chhoot sakte hain, jabki English mein 1-2 hote hain. Iska matlab hai: Hindi chatbot banane par 3-4x zyada API cost! Isliye production mein always token count monitor karo.

3.2 Embeddings

🌐 Embeddings — Meaning ko Numbers Mein Convert Karo

Embedding ek aisi technique hai jo words ya sentences ko high-dimensional numerical vectors mein convert karti hai. In vectors mein words ke semantic relationships encode hote hain.

💡Famous Analogy

Word2Vec ke famous result: King - Man + Woman ≈ Queen
Yeh isliye possible hua kyunki embeddings space mein relationships preserve hote hain. "King" aur "Queen" ke vectors mein jo difference hai, wahi "Man" aur "Woman" ke beech bhi hai!

Word Embeddings 2D Visualization (simplified)
Python — OpenAI Embeddings API 
from openai import OpenAI
import numpy as np

client = OpenAI()

def get_embedding(text):
    return client.embeddings.create(
        input=text,
        model="text-embedding-3-small"
    ).data[0].embedding

def cosine_similarity(v1, v2):
    # Cosine similarity: dot product / (magnitudes ka product)
    return np.dot(v1, v2) / (np.linalg.norm(v1) * np.linalg.norm(v2))

# Test sentences
s1 = "I love machine learning"
s2 = "AI aur deep learning mujhe bahut pasand hai"  
s3 = "I enjoy cooking biryani"

e1, e2, e3 = get_embedding(s1), get_embedding(s2), get_embedding(s3)

print(f"s1 vs s2 (similar topic): {cosine_similarity(e1,e2):.4f}")
# Output: 0.8234 (high — dono AI ke baare mein hain)

print(f"s1 vs s3 (different topic): {cosine_similarity(e1,e3):.4f}")
# Output: 0.2156 (low — ek AI hai, doosra cooking)

print(f"Embedding dimension: {len(e1)}")
# Output: 1536
3.3 Context Window

🪟 Context Window — LLM ki Memory

Context Window wo maximum number of tokens hai jo ek LLM ek baar mein "dekh" sakta hai — yaani process kar sakta hai. Iske baad woh bhool jaata hai!

Context Window — Different Models Comparison
📌Context Window ka Practical Use

System prompt + Chat history + User query + Expected response — yeh sab context window mein fit hone chahiye. Agar context window full ho jaaye, toh model purani baatein "bhool" jaata hai ya error deta hai. Isliye context management production mein ek major challenge hai!

3.4 Hallucinations

👻 Hallucinations — Jhootha Yakeen se Bachna

LLM Hallucination tab hoti hai jab model confident tone mein galat ya made-up information present karta hai. Yeh LLMs ki sabse badi problem hai!

Hallucination ke Types

📚
Factual Hallucination

Galat facts: "Eiffel Tower 1920 mein bana" (actually 1889)

🔗
Source Hallucination

Fake references: Non-existent papers ya books cite karna

🧮
Math Hallucination

Galat calculations: "17×23 = 401" (actually 391)

🏥
Medical Hallucination

Dangerous — galat doses ya treatments suggest karna

Hallucination Kyun Hoti Hai?

1
Training data ka pattern completion

LLM actually "predict next token" karta hai — isse kabhi kabhi plausible-sounding lekin galat patterns generate ho jaate hain.

2
Knowledge cutoff

Model ke training data ke baad ki events ke baare mein model ko kuch pata nahi — phir bhi guess karta hai.

3
Rare knowledge

Jo topics training mein kam baar aaye the, unpe model zyada hallucinate karta hai.

4
Overconfidence

Model ki training mein "I don't know" jaisi responses ko penalize kiya gaya hoga.

💡Hallucination Reduce Karne ke Tips

1. RAG use karo — factual data provide karo context mein
2. Grounding instructions — "Answer only from given context"
3. Temperature kam karo — creativity reduce, factual accuracy increase
4. Chain-of-Thought — step by step sochne se logical errors kam hote hain
5. Self-verification — model se apna answer verify karwao

3.5 Temperature, Top-k, Top-p, Sampling

🌡️ Temperature aur Sampling — Creativity Control

Jab LLM next token predict karta hai, toh usse probability distribution milta hai — har possible next token ki probability. Temperature, Top-k, Top-p — ye parameters decide karte hain ki us distribution se kaisa token sample karein.

Temperature — Creativity Knob

TemperatureEffectUse CaseExample
0.0Always most likely token (deterministic)Factual Q&A, Code"1+1 = 2" always
0.3-0.5Slightly creative, mostly focusedSummaries, Data extractionConsistent but varied
0.7-0.9Balanced creativityChat, Content writingNatural conversation
1.0+Highly creative, sometimes randomPoetry, Creative writingUnexpected metaphors
2.0+Near-random, incoherentNot recommendedGibberish possible
Temperature Effect on Token Probability Distribution

Top-k Sampling

Top-k sampling mein sirf top k most probable tokens ko consider kiya jaata hai, baaki sab ignore. Jaise k=50 means: sirf 50 most likely next words mein se sample karo.

Top-p (Nucleus) Sampling

Top-p mein cumulative probability threshold p use hota hai. Jab tak top tokens ki cumulative probability ≤ p ho, unhe consider karo. Yeh zyada adaptive hai Top-k se.

Top-p: Sum tokens until Σ P(token) ≥ p (e.g., 0.9)
p=0.9 means: Top tokens jo milke 90% probability cover karte hain, unhi mein se sample karo
Python — Temperature & Sampling Parameters with OpenAI 
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

def generate_text(prompt, temperature=0.7, top_p=1.0):
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o-mini",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
        temperature=temperature,  # 0.0 - 2.0
        top_p=top_p,            # 0.1 - 1.0
        max_tokens=100
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content

prompt = "Write a one-line story about AI"

# Low temperature — consistent, focused
print("Temp 0.1:", generate_text(prompt, temperature=0.1))
# Output: "An AI learned to understand human emotions."
# (Almost same output every run)

# High temperature — creative, varied
print("Temp 1.5:", generate_text(prompt, temperature=1.5))
# Output: "The last algorithm dreamed in electric verse."
# (Different every run — more creative!)

# Top-p sampling
print("Top-p 0.5:", generate_text(prompt, temperature=1.0, top_p=0.5))
# More focused — only top 50% probable tokens considered

# BEST PRACTICE: Ek baar mein temperature YA top_p change karo, dono ek saath nahi!
ParameterRangeLow Value EffectHigh Value EffectRecommended
temperature0.0 - 2.0Deterministic, focusedCreative, random0.7 for chat
top_p0.0 - 1.0Very focused nucleusFull vocab possible0.9 default
top_k1 - vocab sizeOnly top 1 (greedy)More diversity40-50 typical
max_tokens1 - context_maxShort responsesLonger responsesTask dependent
🎯Interview Questions — LLM Concepts
  • Temperature aur Top-p mein kya difference hai? Dono ek saath change karna kyun avoid karna chahiye?
  • Context window limits kya hain aur production mein inhe kaise handle karte hain?
  • LLM hallucination kyun hoti hai? Top 3 mitigation strategies kya hain?
  • Word embeddings aur token embeddings mein kya difference hai?
  • "1 token ≈ 4 characters" — yeh estimate kab valid hai aur kab nahi?
📝 Chapter 3 Summary — LLM Concepts
  • Tokens: LLM ki basic currency — 1 word ≈ 1.3 tokens (English) | Hindi mein 3-4x zyada
  • Embeddings: Semantic meaning ko vectors mein encode karna — similar meaning = close vectors
  • Context Window: LLM ek time mein kitna "dekh" sakta hai — 16K se 1M tokens range
  • Hallucinations: Confident but wrong answers — RAG aur grounding se reduce karo
  • Temperature: 0 = deterministic, 1+ = creative | Top-p = nucleus sampling
AI Agent Engineering Masterbook — Part 5 | Chapter 3 LLM Concepts
Chapter 4 · Advanced LLM Topics

🚀 Advanced LLM Techniques

MoE, LoRA, RLHF — wo techniques jo LLMs ko aur powerful banati hain

4.1 MoE — Mixture of Experts

🎪 Mixture of Experts (MoE) — Specialization ka Jadoo

MoE ek aisi architecture hai jisme ek bade model ke andar multiple "expert" networks hote hain. Har input ke liye, ek router decide karta hai ki kaun se experts use karne hain. Sab experts ek saath nahi chalte — sirf kuch selected experts activate hote hain!

💡Real World Analogy — Hospital

Ek hospital mein alag-alag doctors hote hain — Cardiologist, Neurologist, Orthopedist etc. Jab patient aata hai, toh receptionist (router) decide karta hai ki konse doctor se milna hai. Har patient ke liye sab doctors kaam nahi karte — sirf relevant doctor. MoE bhi aisa hi kaam karta hai!

Mixture of Experts Architecture
PropertyDense Model (e.g., GPT-3)MoE Model (e.g., Mixtral)
Parameters175B — sab active~46B active, 141B total
ComputationFull model har token peSirf selected experts
SpeedSlower (per token)3-4x faster inference
TrainingExpensive but simpleComplex but efficient
QualityHighSimilar or better!
4.2 Quantization

📦 Quantization — Size Chhota Karo, Speed Badhao

LLMs mein billions of parameters hote hain, jo originally FP32 (32-bit floating point) mein store hote hain. Quantization in parameters ko lower precision (8-bit, 4-bit, even 2-bit) mein convert karta hai — size aur memory dramatically reduce ho jaati hai!

✅ Quantization ke Fayde
  • Model size 4-8x chhota hota hai
  • Inference speed 2-4x faster
  • GPU memory kam chahiye
  • Consumer hardware par bhi run ho sakta hai
  • Cloud costs significantly kam
❌ Quantization ke Nuksan
  • Thodi accuracy loss (usually minimal)
  • Complex tasks mein quality thodi drop
  • Integer overflow edge cases
  • 2-bit jaise extreme cases degrade karte hain
PrecisionBitsSize (7B model)RAM RequiredQuality
FP3232-bit~28 GB32+ GBBaseline (100%)
FP16 / BF1616-bit~14 GB16+ GB~99%
INT8 (Q8)8-bit~7 GB8+ GB~98%
INT4 (Q4)4-bit~3.5 GB6+ GB~95%
INT2 (Q2)2-bit~1.8 GB4+ GB~85% ⚠️
Python — Quantized Model Loading (bitsandbytes + HuggingFace) 
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, BitsAndBytesConfig
import torch

# 4-bit quantization config define karo
bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
    load_in_4bit=True,                    # 4-bit quantization
    bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",              # NormalFloat4 (better quality)
    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,  # BF16 for computation
    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True          # Double quantization for extra compression
)

model_id = "meta-llama/Llama-3-8B-Instruct"

# Quantized model load karo
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
    model_id,
    quantization_config=bnb_config,
    device_map="auto"  # Automatically GPU/CPU split
)

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)

# Memory check karo
print(f"Model footprint: {model.get_memory_footprint() / 1e9:.2f} GB")
# Normal: ~16GB (BF16) → Quantized: ~4GB (4-bit) — 4x reduction!
4.3 Fine-tuning & Instruction Tuning

🎯 Fine-tuning — LLM ko Apna Karo

Pre-training mein LLM internet ki vast data par train hota hai. Fine-tuning mein hum uss pre-trained model ko apne specific task ya domain ke liye further train karte hain.

Pre-trained LLM
(GPT, Llama, Mistral)
+
Task-specific
Dataset
→
Fine-tuning
Process
→
Fine-tuned
Model

Instruction Tuning — Commands Sikhana

Base LLMs ko instructions follow karne mein problem hoti hai — woh text complete karte hain, instructions follow nahi karte. Instruction Tuning model ko sikhata hai ki "jab user instruction de, toh accordingly respond karo."

TypeKya HaiExampleModels
Domain Fine-tuningSpecific domain data par trainMedical LLM, Legal LLMBioGPT, LegalBERT
Instruction TuningInstruction-following sikhana"Summarize this article"GPT-3.5, Llama-instruct
Task Fine-tuningEk specific taskSentiment Analysis onlyBERT fine-tuned
Chat Fine-tuningConversational style sikhanaChatGPT-style responsesOpenChat, Zephyr
4.4 RLHF — Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

👥 RLHF — Human se Seekhna

RLHF woh technique hai jisne ChatGPT ko "actually helpful" banaya! Isme human feedback use karke LLM ko train kiya jaata hai taaki woh human preferences ke according behave kare.

RLHF Pipeline — Teen Steps
4.5 Knowledge Distillation

🧪 Knowledge Distillation — Bade se Chhote Mein

Distillation mein ek large "teacher" model ki knowledge ek small "student" model mein transfer ki jaati hai. Student model teacher ki soft outputs (probability distributions) se seekhta hai, na sirf hard labels se.

Large Teacher Model
(GPT-4, 175B params)
→ soft labels →
Training Process
(KL Divergence Loss)
→
Small Student Model
(DistilBERT, 66M params)
📌Famous Distillation Examples

DistilBERT = BERT (110M params) → 66M params, 60% faster, 97% performance
TinyLlama = Llama-2 (7B) → 1.1B params
Phi series (Microsoft) = Distilled small but capable models
Gemini Nano = Gemini ka mobile-optimized version

4.6 LoRA & PEFT

🔧 LoRA aur PEFT — Smart Fine-tuning

Full fine-tuning mein problem ye hai ki millions of GPU memory chahiye aur sab parameters update hote hain — bahut expensive! PEFT (Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning) methods sirf kuch parameters update karte hain baaki frozen rakhte hain.

LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) — Smart Mathematics

W' = W + ΔW = W + BA
W = original frozen weight matrix (large) | B, A = low-rank matrices (tiny, trainable)
💡LoRA Intuition

Socho ek 1000×1000 matrix ko update karna padta hai = 1,000,000 parameters.
LoRA kehta hai: Ise 1000×r aur r×1000 matrices se represent karo (jahan r=8 ya 16).
Ab sirf 2×1000×8 = 16,000 parameters train karne padenge — 62x less!
Full model quality ka 95%+ quality milti hai, cost fraction mein!

LoRA — Weight Update Visualization
Python — LoRA Fine-tuning with PEFT + TRL 
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model, TaskType
from trl import SFTTrainer
from datasets import load_dataset

# 1. Base model load karo
model_id = "mistralai/Mistral-7B-v0.1"
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id)
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)

# 2. LoRA configuration define karo
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r=16,                    # Rank — small = less params, fast
    lora_alpha=32,             # Scaling factor (2*r is common)
    target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],  # Attention layers
    lora_dropout=0.05,
    bias="none",
    task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM
)

# 3. LoRA apply karo
peft_model = get_peft_model(model, lora_config)

# Trainable parameters check karo
peft_model.print_trainable_parameters()
# Output: trainable params: 4,194,304 || all params: 7,246,419,968 (0.06% trainable!)

# 4. Dataset aur Trainer setup
dataset = load_dataset("your_custom_dataset", split="train")

trainer = SFTTrainer(
    model=peft_model,
    train_dataset=dataset,
    dataset_text_field="text",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    max_seq_length=512
)

trainer.train()
peft_model.save_pretrained("./my-fine-tuned-lora")
PEFT MethodHow it WorksBest ForTrainable Params
LoRALow-rank matrices add karo attention layers meinGeneral fine-tuning0.1-1%
QLoRALoRA + 4-bit quantized base modelLimited GPU memory0.06%
Prefix TuningTrainable prefix tokens add karoGeneration tasks<0.1%
Prompt TuningSoft prompt tokens sikhaoInstruction tuning<0.01%
AdapterSmall networks layers ke beech add karoMultiple tasks1-3%
🎯Interview Questions — Advanced LLM Topics
  • MoE architecture kaise kaam karta hai? Sparse aur dense models mein kya tradeoffs hain?
  • INT4 quantization mein exactly kya hota hai? Quality loss kyun hoti hai?
  • RLHF ke 3 steps explain karo. PPO algorithm ka role kya hai?
  • LoRA mathematically kaise kaam karta hai? r (rank) ki value kaise decide karte hain?
  • Full fine-tuning vs LoRA vs QLoRA — kab kaunsa choose karo?
  • Knowledge distillation mein "soft labels" kya hote hain? Hard labels se better kyun hain?
💪 Mini Exercises — Chapter 4
  1. Ek 7B model ko 4-bit quantize karo aur original size se compare karo. Inference speed ka difference measure karo.
  2. LoRA rank r=4, r=8, r=16, r=32 ke saath fine-tune karo aur quality vs training time compare karo.
  3. Ek simple reward model banao jo helpful vs unhelpful responses classify kare.
  4. MoE concept implement karo: 3 "expert" MLPs banao + ek router network jo top-2 experts select kare.
  5. DistilBERT aur BERT-base ka performance compare karo sentiment analysis task par.
AI Agent Engineering Masterbook — Part 5 | Chapter 4 Advanced LLM Topics
Chapter 5 · Popular Models

🌍 Popular AI Models — Battlefield of Giants

GPT, Llama, Gemini, Claude, Mistral — AI duniya ke superhero models

Aaj AI mein kaafi saari companies apne powerful language models release kar rahi hain. Har ek ke apne strengths, weaknesses, aur unique approaches hain. Ek AI engineer ko inke baare mein deeply pata hona chahiye — kyunki projects mein sahi model choose karna ek critical decision hai!

🤖
GPT-4 / GPT-4o
by OpenAI
Sabse popular commercial model. Multimodal (text + image + audio). 128K context. Best for enterprise applications.
GPT-4o Multimodal API
🦙
Llama 3.1 / 3.3
by Meta AI
Open-source ka king. 8B se 405B tak. Commercial use allowed. Fine-tuning ke liye best choice. Self-hosting possible.
Open Source Self-host 128K ctx
💎
Gemini 1.5 / 2.0
by Google DeepMind
World's largest context window — 1M+ tokens. Natively multimodal from ground up. Best for long document analysis.
1M Context Multimodal Google APIs
🎭
Claude 3.5 / 3.7
by Anthropic
Safety-focused model. Constitutional AI training. Excellent at coding, analysis, nuanced writing. 200K context window.
Safety First 200K ctx Coding
🌪️
Mistral / Mixtral
by Mistral AI
European efficiency champion. Small size, big performance. Mixtral 8x7B uses MoE. Best performance-per-dollar ratio.
Efficient MoE Open Weights
OpenAI GPT Series

🤖 OpenAI GPT — The Pioneer

OpenAI ne 2018 mein pehla GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) launch kiya. Tab se yeh series continuously evolve ho rahi hai. ChatGPT ne November 2022 mein world mein AI revolution la diya — sirf 5 dinon mein 1 million users!

ModelYearParamsContextKey Innovation
GPT-12018117M512 tokensPehla large pre-trained transformer
GPT-220191.5B1K tokens"Too dangerous to release" — coherent text
GPT-32020175B4K tokensFew-shot learning, InstructGPT
ChatGPT2022~175B4-16KRLHF — conversational AI
GPT-42023~1.7T (est)128KMultimodal, MoE architecture
GPT-4o2024Unknown128KOmni — text+vision+audio native
o1 / o32024-25Unknown200KChain-of-thought reasoning model
Python — OpenAI GPT-4o API 
from openai import OpenAI
import base64

client = OpenAI()

# ---- Basic Text Generation ----
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful Hindi tutor."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Machine Learning kya hota hai? Simple mein explain karo"}
    ],
    temperature=0.7,
    max_tokens=500
)

print(response.choices[0].message.content)
print("\n--- Token Usage ---")
print("Input tokens:", response.usage.prompt_tokens)
print("Output tokens:", response.usage.completion_tokens)

# ---- Vision (Image Analysis) ----
response_vision = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
            {"type": "text", "text": "Is image mein kya ho raha hai?"},
            {"type": "image_url", "image_url": {"url": "https://example.com/image.jpg"}}
        ]
    }]
)
print(response_vision.choices[0].message.content)
Meta Llama

🦙 Meta Llama — Open-Source ka Badshah

Meta ne Llama (Large Language Model Meta AI) series ke through open-source AI democratization ki. Llama 3 release ke baad, yeh GPT-4 level performance open-source mein available ho gaya. Llama 3.1 405B GPT-4 se comparable performance deta hai!

📌Llama kyun Special Hai?

Open weights: Download karke apne server par chala sako
Fine-tuning: Apne data par customize karo
Privacy: Data bahar nahi jaata
Cost: Ek baar GPU invest karo, unlimited inference!
Commercial: Llama 3 commercial use allowed (upto certain scale)

Python — Llama 3 with Ollama (Local) 
# Pehle install: pip install ollama
# Aur Ollama CLI: ollama pull llama3.1
import ollama

# Simple generation
response = ollama.chat(
    model="llama3.1",
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Python mein fibonacci sequence likho"
    }]
)
print(response["message"]["content"])

# ---- HuggingFace se directly ----
from transformers import pipeline

pipe = pipeline(
    "text-generation",
    model="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
    device_map="auto"
)

messages = [
    {"role": "system", "content": "Tum ek expert Python developer ho."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "Binary search algorithm explain karo."}
]

output = pipe(messages, max_new_tokens=512)
print(output[0]["generated_text"][-1]["content"])
Google Gemini

💎 Google Gemini — Multimodal Native

Google DeepMind ka Gemini pehla model hai jo scratch se truly multimodal design ke saath banaya gaya — text, image, audio, video, code sab kuch natively handle karta hai. Gemini 1.5 ka 1 million token context window industry mein sabse bada hai!

Python — Google Gemini API 
import google.generativeai as genai
from PIL import Image

# API setup
genai.configure(api_key="YOUR_GEMINI_API_KEY")

# Text generation
model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro")
response = model.generate_content("AI ka bhavishya kya hai? Hinglish mein batao")
print(response.text)

# Multimodal — image + text
img = Image.open("chart.png")
response = model.generate_content([
    "Is chart ko analyze karo aur trends batao",
    img
])
print(response.text)

# Long context — 1 million tokens!
long_model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro-002")
with open("massive_document.txt", "r") as f:
    massive_doc = f.read()  # 500+ pages OK!

response = long_model.generate_content(
    f"{massive_doc}\n\nIs document mein key themes kya hain?"
)
print(response.text)
Anthropic Claude

🎭 Anthropic Claude — Safety aur Intelligence

Anthropic ek AI safety company hai jisne Claude create kiya. Claude Constitutional AI (CAI) technique se train kiya gaya hai — model ke andar ek "constitution" hota hai jo define karta hai ki model ko kaise behave karna chahiye.

📌Constitutional AI Kya Hai?

Normal RLHF mein human raters directly feedback dete hain. CAI mein:
1. Model ek response generate karta hai
2. Model khud apne response ko pre-defined principles ke basis par critique karta hai
3. Model revised response likhta hai
4. Is revised response se model train hota hai
Result: Safer, more helpful, honest model!

Python — Anthropic Claude API 
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key="YOUR_KEY")

# Basic message
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system="Tum ek expert AI engineer ho. Hinglish mein helpful responses do.",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Mujhe RAG architecture samjhao"}
    ]
)
print(message.content[0].text)

# Vision - Image analysis
import base64
with open("diagram.png", "rb") as f:
    img_data = base64.b64encode(f.read()).decode("utf-8")

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
    max_tokens=500,
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
            {"type": "image", "source": {
                "type": "base64",
                "media_type": "image/png",
                "data": img_data
            }},
            {"type": "text", "text": "Is architecture diagram ko explain karo"}
        ]
    }]
)
print(response.content[0].text)
Mistral AI

🌪️ Mistral — European Efficiency Champion

Mistral AI ek French startup hai jo 2023 mein banai gayi. Inke models small size mein bada punch deliver karte hain. Mixtral 8x7B ne MoE architecture use karke GPT-3.5 level performance achieve ki sirf 47B total parameters mein!

Python — Mistral API 
from mistralai import Mistral

client = Mistral(api_key="YOUR_MISTRAL_KEY")

# Mixtral 8x7B — cost-effective powerhouse
response = client.chat.complete(
    model="mixtral-8x7b-instruct",
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Code review karo is Python function ka"}
    ]
)
print(response.choices[0].message.content)

# Codestral — code-specific model
code_response = client.chat.complete(
    model="codestral-latest",
    messages=[{
        "role": "user",
        "content": "FastAPI mein JWT authentication implement karo"
    }]
)
print(code_response.choices[0].message.content)
Model Comparison

⚖️ Model Comparison — Kab Kaun?

Use CaseRecommended ModelWhy?
General chat applicationGPT-4o / Claude 3.5Best quality, reliable API
Cost-sensitive productionMixtral 8x7B / GPT-3.5High quality, low cost
Long document processingGemini 1.5 Pro1M token context
Privacy / self-hostingLlama 3.1 / Mistral 7BOpen weights, on-premise
Code generationClaude 3.5 / CodestralStrong coding ability
Fine-tuning projectLlama 3.1 8B / Mistral 7BOpen weights + efficient
Multimodal (image/audio)Gemini 1.5 / GPT-4oNative multimodal
Safety-critical appsClaude 3.5 SonnetConstitutional AI training
Research/experimentationAny open-source (HuggingFace)Full control, no cost
🎯Interview Questions — Popular Models
  • GPT-4 aur GPT-4o mein kya fark hai? "o" ka kya matlab hai?
  • Llama 3 ki licensing kaise hai? Kab commercial use allowed hai?
  • Gemini 1.5 ka 1M context window practically kaise use karte hain?
  • Constitutional AI kya hai aur RLHF se kaise different hai?
  • Ek startup ke liye GPT-4 vs Llama 3 — kaunsa choose karoge aur kyun?
  • Mixtral 8x7B, GPT-3.5 se kaisi compare karta hai cost-quality tradeoff mein?
💪 Mini Exercises — Chapter 5
  1. Ek Python script banao jo ek hi prompt ko GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, aur Gemini 1.5 par run kare aur responses compare kare.
  2. Llama 3.1 ko Ollama se locally run karo aur latency measure karo. GPT-4o API se compare karo.
  3. Gemini 1.5 ka 100K+ token context test karo: ek long PDF pass karo aur specific facts query karo.
  4. Mistral 7B ko HuggingFace se load karo aur 4-bit quantized version se compare karo speed/quality mein.
  5. Ek simple chatbot banao jo user ke query ke basis par automatically sahi model choose kare (routing).
Glossary

📖 Glossary — Part 5 ke Key Terms

TokenizationText ko chhote units (tokens) mein todna — BPE modern LLMs ka standard method
StemmingWord ka approximate root form nikalna — fast but rough (Porter, Snowball algorithms)
LemmatizationGrammar-correct dictionary root form — stems se zyada accurate (SpaCy use karo)
POS TaggingParts of Speech assign karna — Noun/Verb/Adj/etc. har word ko
NERNamed Entity Recognition — text mein Person, Org, Location detect karna
Transformer2017 "Attention Is All You Need" paper ka architecture — modern LLMs ka base
Self-AttentionEk sequence ke words apne hi sequence ke dusre words par attention dete hain
Multi-Head AttentionMultiple parallel attention heads — alag-alag relationships capture karte hain
EmbeddingWords/sentences ko dense numerical vectors mein convert karna
Context WindowLLM ek baar mein maximum kitne tokens process kar sakta hai
HallucinationLLM ka confident lekin galat information generate karna
TemperatureOutput randomness control — 0=deterministic, 1+=creative
Top-p (Nucleus)Cumulative probability threshold sampling — adaptive token selection
MoEMixture of Experts — sparse activation se efficient large models
QuantizationModel weights ko lower precision mein convert karna (FP32→INT4)
Fine-tuningPre-trained model ko specific task/domain par further train karna
RLHFReinforcement Learning from Human Feedback — ChatGPT-level helpfulness ki key
DistillationLarge teacher model ki knowledge small student model mein transfer karna
LoRALow-Rank Adaptation — efficient fine-tuning (0.06% params train karo)
PEFTParameter Efficient Fine-Tuning — LoRA, QLoRA, Adapters etc. ka umbrella term
BPEByte Pair Encoding — GPT-style subword tokenization algorithm
Positional EncodingSin/Cos signals jo transformer ko word order batate hain
📝 Part 5 — Complete Summary
  • NLP Foundation: Tokenization (BPE), Stemming/Lemmatization, POS Tagging, NER — ye sab raw text ko processable banate hain
  • Transformer Revolution: 2017 mein Q,K,V attention ne sab kuch badla — parallel processing, no more RNN
  • LLM Concepts: Tokens (currency), Embeddings (meaning in vectors), Context Window (memory limit), Temperature (creativity)
  • Hallucinations: LLMs confident but wrong ho sakte hain — RAG + grounding se mitigate karo
  • Advanced Techniques: MoE (sparse activation), Quantization (size reduction), RLHF (human alignment), LoRA (efficient fine-tuning)
  • Model Landscape: GPT (commercial), Llama (open-source), Gemini (multimodal+long context), Claude (safety), Mistral (efficient)
  • Production Choice: Use case ke hisaab se model choose karo — cost, privacy, context, quality sab factors hain
AI Agent Engineering Masterbook — Part 5 | Chapter 5 Popular Models & Final Summary

🔁 Quick Revision — Part 5 ke Saare Important Points

🔤
Tokenization

BPE = modern standard. 1 token ≈ 0.75 words (English). Hindi mein 3-4x zyada tokens!

✂️
Stemming vs Lemma

Stemming = fast but rough. Lemmatization = accurate, grammar-correct. SpaCy for lemma.

👁️
Attention Formula

Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(Q·Kᵀ/√dₖ)·V. Q=query, K=key, V=value.

🎭
Multi-Head

8-16 parallel heads. Har head alag relationship pakadta hai. Concatenate + project.

🔄
Encoder vs Decoder

Encoder=understanding (BERT). Decoder=generation (GPT). Both=translation (T5).

📍
Positional Encoding

Sin/Cos signals. Without this, model ko word order pata nahi. Input embedding mein add.

🌐
Embeddings

King - Man + Woman ≈ Queen. Similar meaning = close vectors. 1536 dims for OpenAI.

🪟
Context Window

GPT-4=128K, Claude=200K, Gemini=1M. Full hone par model bhool jaata hai!

👻
Hallucinations

Confident but wrong. RAG + grounding + low temperature se reduce karo.

🌡️
Temperature

0=same always. 0.7=balanced. 1.5+=creative. Top-p=nucleus sampling.

🎪
MoE

Router selects top-2 experts. Sparse activation. Mixtral = MoE based. 3-4x faster.

📦
Quantization

FP32→INT4 = 8x smaller. Q4 ≈ 95% quality. bitsandbytes library use karo.

👥
RLHF

3 steps: SFT → Reward Model → PPO. Human feedback se alignment. ChatGPT ka secret.

🔧
LoRA

W' = W + BA. r=16 = 0.06% trainable params. 1750x less than full fine-tuning!

🧪
Distillation

Teacher → Student. DistilBERT = 60% faster, 97% quality. Soft labels better hain.

🌟Remember This — Part 5 Core Mantra

NLP → Transformer → LLM → Fine-tune → Deploy
Yahi journey hai ek simple text processing se world's most powerful AI tak.

Text ko tokens mein toro, tokens ko embeddings mein convert karo, Transformer se context samjho, RLHF se helpful banao, LoRA se customize karo — aur duniya badal do! 🚀
