🔮
Sem. Kernel
Enterprise
🌾
Haystack
NLP Pipelines
Har framework ka apna specialty area hota hai — ek project me multiple frameworks bhi use ho sakti hain

Framework Selection Mindset

Framework choose karte waqt yeh questions pucho:

  • Kya mujhe simple chains chahiye ya complex stateful workflows? → LangChain vs LangGraph
  • Kya mera task mainly document retrieval / RAG hai? → LlamaIndex
  • Kya mujhe multiple AI agents milkar kaam karte chahiye? → CrewAI ya AutoGen
  • Kya mera organization Microsoft stack use karta hai? → Semantic Kernel
  • Kya mujhe production-grade NLP pipeline chahiye? → Haystack

Chapter 2 — Core Concepts: Chains, Agents, Memory, Tools

Sabse pehle, hume yeh samajhna hoga ki sabhi frameworks mein 4 core building blocks hote hain — chahe naam alag ho, concept same hai.

⛓
Chains
Steps ki sequence — ek ka output doosre ka input
🤖
Agents
Autonomous entities jo decide karte hain kya action lena hai
🧠
Memory
Past context store karna aur retrieve karna
🔧
Tools
External functions jo agent call kar sakta hai
📊
Query Engines
Documents se structured answers nikalna
🎭
Orchestration
Multiple agents ko coordinate karna

1. Chains — Kya hain aur kaise kaam karte hain?

Chain ek pipeline hai jahan ek step ka output directly doosre step ka input banta hai. Jaise assembly line mein har station ek kaam karta hai — chain mein bhi ऐसा hi hota hai.

⛓ Chain Flow — Step by Step
📝 User Input
→
✂️ Prompt Template
→
🤖 LLM Call
→
📤 Output Parser
→
✅ Final Answer
LangChain Expression Language (LCEL): chain = prompt | llm | parser
Python — Simple Chain (LangChain)
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI from langchain_core.output_parsers import StrOutputParser # Step 1: Prompt Template banao prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([ ("system", "Tum ek helpful AI assistant ho."), ("user", "{question}") ]) # Step 2: LLM initialize karo llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o", temperature=0.7) # Step 3: Output parser parser = StrOutputParser() # Step 4: Chain banao — LCEL syntax (pipe operator) chain = prompt | llm | parser # Step 5: Chain invoke karo result = chain.invoke({"question": "Python kya hai?"}) print(result) # Output: "Python ek high-level, interpreted programming language hai..."

2. Agents — Autonomous Decision Making

Chain mein flow fixed hota hai — agent mein LLM khud decide karta hai ki agla step kya hoga. Agent ko tools diye jaate hain, aur wo observe → think → act loop follow karta hai.

🤖 ReAct Agent Loop — Observe → Think → Act
👤 User Query
🧠 LLM "Mujhe kya karna chahiye?"
🔧 Tool A
Search
OR
🔧 Tool B
Calculator
OR
📝 Direct Answer
👁 Observation (Tool Result)
Loop continues jab tak answer nahi milta...
✅ Final Answer to User

3. Memory — Context Yaad Rakhna

Memory TypeKya Store Karta HaiExample Use
Buffer MemoryPoori conversation historyChatbot jo pichla context yaad rakhe
Summary MemoryCompressed summary of conversationLong conversations ke liye
Vector MemorySemantically similar past messagesRelevant context retrieve karna
Entity MemoryKey entities (people, places)"Rahul ka phone number kya tha?"
External MemoryDatabase / file mein persistSessions ke beech yaad rakhna

4. Tools — Agent ki Superpowers

Tools wo functions hain jo agent call kar sakta hai LLM ke baahar jaake. Jaise ek student ko library, calculator, aur internet access milta hai — agent ko tools milte hain.

🔍 Search Tool
  • Google/Bing search
  • Wikipedia lookup
  • News search
🧮 Calculator Tool
  • Math operations
  • Python code execution
  • Data analysis
📁 File Tools
  • Read/write files
  • PDF reading
  • CSV processing
🌐 API Tools
  • HTTP requests
  • Database queries
  • Weather/Maps APIs
💻 Code Tools
  • Code execution
  • Bash commands
  • Git operations
🧠 Memory Tools
  • Vector DB search
  • Fact retrieval
  • Context injection
💡 Pro Tip
Tools likhte waqt clear docstrings likho — LLM yahi padh ke decide karta hai ki kab kaunsa tool use karna hai. Agar docstring unclear hai, agent wrong tool use karega.
Framework 1

Chapter 3 — LangChain 🦜

Beginner Friendly Intermediate Production Ready
🦜
LangChain
The most popular AI application framework — LLM pe based applications banane ka goto library

LangChain 2022 mein Harrison Chase ne banaya tha. Yeh ek open-source framework hai jo LLMs ko external data sources aur tools ke saath easily connect karne deta hai. Aaj ke date mein yeh sabse zyada use hone wala AI framework hai jiske 90,000+ GitHub stars hain.

LangChain ka Architecture

🏗 LangChain Architecture — Layered Design
🎯
Application Layer
Chatbots, RAG apps, Agents, Automation workflows
⛓
LangChain Chains & Agents
LCEL, AgentExecutor, Tools, Memory, Callbacks
🔌
LangChain Integrations
OpenAI, Anthropic, Pinecone, ChromaDB, Tavily, 500+ integrations
⚙️
LangChain Core (langchain-core)
Base classes: Runnable, BaseLanguageModel, BaseTool, BaseMemory

LangChain ke Key Components

1. LCEL — LangChain Expression Language

LCEL LangChain ka modern way hai components ko compose karne ka. Pipe operator (|) se chains banate hain — bilkul Unix commands ki tarah.

Python — LCEL Complete Example
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate from langchain_core.output_parsers import StrOutputParser, JsonOutputParser from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableParallel, RunnablePassthrough # === Basic Chain === basic_chain = ( ChatPromptTemplate.from_template("Topic: {topic} ke baare mein batao") | ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini") | StrOutputParser() ) # === Parallel Chains — dono kaam ek saath === parallel_chain = RunnableParallel( summary=basic_chain, keywords=( ChatPromptTemplate.from_template( "{topic} ke 5 keywords JSON mein do: {{keywords: [...]}}" ) | ChatOpenAI() | JsonOutputParser() ) ) # Invoke karo result = parallel_chain.invoke({"topic": "Machine Learning"}) print(result["summary"]) # ML ki summary print(result["keywords"]) # {"keywords": ["neural", "training", ...]} # === Streaming support === for chunk in basic_chain.stream({"topic": "AI Agents"}): print(chunk, end="", flush=True)

2. LangChain Agents with Tools

Python — Tool-Using Agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI from langchain.agents import create_tool_calling_agent, AgentExecutor from langchain_core.tools import tool from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate # Custom Tool banana — decorator use karo @tool def calculate_area(length: float, width: float) -> float: """Kisi bhi rectangle ka area calculate karta hai.""" return length * width @tool def get_weather(city: str) -> str: """Kisi city ka weather batata hai.""" # Real implementation mein API call hogi return f"{city} mein aaj 28°C, sunny weather hai" # Tools list tools = [calculate_area, get_weather] # Agent prompt prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([ ("system", "Tum ek helpful assistant ho. Diye gaye tools use karo."), ("placeholder", "{chat_history}"), ("human", "{input}"), ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"), ]) # LLM + Agent + Executor llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o") agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, prompt) executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True) # Agent ko kaam do result = executor.invoke({ "input": "Mumbai ka weather kya hai aur 10x5 ka area kya hoga?", "chat_history": [] }) # Output: "Mumbai mein 28°C hai aur area 50 square units hai"

3. RAG with LangChain

Python — RAG Pipeline with LangChain
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI, OpenAIEmbeddings from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma from langchain_community.document_loaders import PyPDFLoader from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate from langchain_core.runnables import RunnablePassthrough from langchain_core.output_parsers import StrOutputParser # Step 1: Document load karo loader = PyPDFLoader("my_document.pdf") docs = loader.load() # Step 2: Chunks mein todo splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=500, chunk_overlap=50 ) chunks = splitter.split_documents(docs) # Step 3: Vector store mein store karo vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents( chunks, OpenAIEmbeddings() ) retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 3}) # Step 4: RAG Prompt rag_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_template(""" Context: {context} Question: {question} Answer the question using ONLY the context above. """) # Step 5: Complete RAG Chain rag_chain = ( {"context": retriever, "question": RunnablePassthrough()} | rag_prompt | ChatOpenAI() | StrOutputParser() ) # Query karo print(rag_chain.invoke("Document mein kya likha hai?"))
🎤 Interview Questions — LangChain
Q1
LangChain mein LCEL kya hai aur iska fayda kya hai? (Pipe operator ka concept explain karo)
Q2
LangChain Chain aur Agent mein kya difference hai?
Q3
AgentExecutor ka kaam kya hota hai? Internally kaise kaam karta hai?
Q4
LangChain mein RAG pipeline kaise banate hain? Steps explain karo.
Q5
Callbacks aur Tracing LangChain mein kaise use hote hain?
⚠️ Common Mistakes — LangChain
  • Deprecated LLMChain use karna — LCEL use karo
  • Tool docstrings vague likhna — LLM tool wrong use karega
  • Memory bina stream kiye use karna — async handling miss ho jaata hai
  • Unnecessary chaining — zyada steps chain slow karti hai
Framework 2

Chapter 4 — LangGraph 🕸

Intermediate Advanced Production Ready
🕸
LangGraph
Stateful, multi-actor applications — Graph-based agent orchestration

LangGraph LangChain team ka ek powerful extension hai jo complex, stateful workflows banane deta hai. Jahan LangChain linear chains banata hai, LangGraph cyclic graphs banata hai — matlab agent loop mein kaam kar sakta hai, decisions le sakta hai, aur state maintain kar sakta hai.

LangGraph vs LangChain — Kab Kya Use Karein?

FeatureLangChainLangGraph
Flow TypeLinear / DAGCyclic Graphs (loops supported)
State ManagementLimitedFirst-class — TypedDict based
Human-in-loopDifficultBuilt-in (interrupt, breakpoints)
Conditional RoutingBasicPowerful conditional edges
DebuggingCallbacksLangSmith Studio visual debugger
Use CaseSimple agents, RAGComplex multi-step agents, workflows

LangGraph Core Concepts

📊
State
TypedDict — graph ki shared memory
📦
Nodes
Functions jo state ko process karte hain
➡️
Edges
Nodes ke beech connections
🔀
Conditional Edges
Dynamic routing — kaun sa node next?
⏸
Checkpoints
State save karna — resume possible
🛑
Interrupts
Human approval lena mid-execution
🕸 LangGraph — ReAct Agent Graph Structure

LangGraph Complete Code Example

Python — LangGraph ReAct Agent
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode, tools_condition from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI from langchain_core.tools import tool from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages # === Step 1: State define karo === class AgentState(TypedDict): # messages list — Annotated[list, add_messages] se auto-merge hoga messages: Annotated[list, add_messages] # === Step 2: Tools define karo === @tool def search_web(query: str) -> str: """Web pe search karta hai kisi topic ke baare mein.""" return f"Search result for '{query}': Sample data..." tools = [search_web] # === Step 3: LLM ko tools bind karo === llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o").bind_tools(tools) # === Step 4: Nodes define karo === def agent_node(state: AgentState): """Main agent jo LLM call karta hai""" response = llm.invoke(state["messages"]) return {"messages": [response]} # ToolNode automatically tools execute karta hai tool_node = ToolNode(tools) # === Step 5: Graph banao === graph = StateGraph(AgentState) # Nodes add karo graph.add_node("agent", agent_node) graph.add_node("tools", tool_node) # Edges add karo graph.add_edge(START, "agent") graph.add_conditional_edges( "agent", tools_condition, # tool_calls hai → "tools", warna → END ) graph.add_edge("tools", "agent") # tool ke baad wapas agent # === Step 6: Compile & Run === app = graph.compile() result = app.invoke({ "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "AI agents kya hote hain?"}] }) print(result["messages"][-1].content)

Human-in-the-Loop with LangGraph

Python — Human Approval Checkpoint
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver # Checkpointer — state save karta hai memory = MemorySaver() # Compile with interrupt — tool se pehle human approval app = graph.compile( checkpointer=memory, interrupt_before=["tools"] # tool run se PEHLE ruko ) config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "session_1"}} # Step 1: Pehli run — tool call pe ruk jaega result = app.invoke({ "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Search AI trends"}] }, config=config) # Step 2: Human check karo state = app.get_state(config) print("Agent iska tool use karna chahta hai:") print(state.values["messages"][-1].tool_calls) # Step 3: Approve karo — None input se continue hoga approval = input("Approve? (y/n): ") if approval == "y": final = app.invoke(None, config=config) print(final["messages"][-1].content)
🧠 Remember This — LangGraph
LangGraph ka power uski state management aur cycles mein hai. Jab bhi tumhara agent ko loop karna ho, state maintain karna ho, ya human approval chahiye ho — LangGraph use karo. Simple linear tasks ke liye LangChain kaafi hai.
🎤 Interview Questions — LangGraph
Q1
LangGraph mein State, Node, aur Edge kya hote hain? Ek example do.
Q2
Conditional edges kaise kaam karte hain? tools_condition explain karo.
Q3
Human-in-the-loop LangGraph mein kaise implement karte hain?
Q4
LangGraph checkpointing kya hai? Production mein kyun important hai?
Framework 3

Chapter 5 — LlamaIndex 🦙

Beginner Friendly Intermediate Production Ready
🦙
LlamaIndex
Data framework for LLMs — RAG aur document intelligence ka best tool

LlamaIndex (pehle GPT Index) Jerry Liu ne banaya tha. Yeh specifically document-heavy applications ke liye design kiya gaya hai — jahan aapko PDFs, databases, APIs se data LLM ke saath connect karna ho. LangChain general purpose hai, LlamaIndex data retrieval ka specialist hai.

LlamaIndex Architecture

🏗 LlamaIndex Data Flow
📄
Data Sources
PDF, CSV, DB, API, Web
⚙️
Data Loader
Parse & Chunk
🗂
Index
Vector/Tree/List
🔍
Query Engine
Retrieval + Synthesis
💬
LLM Response
Context-aware Answer

LlamaIndex Core Components

ComponentKya karta haiExample
SimpleDirectoryReaderFolder se documents load karta haiPDFs, TXTs, DOCXs auto-parse
VectorStoreIndexDocuments ko vectors mein store karta haiSemantic search enable karta hai
QueryEngineUser query pe documents se answer leta haiPDF se Q&A
ChatEngineConversational interface with memoryDocument chatbot
RetrieverQueryEngineCustom retrieval + response synthesisHybrid search
SubQuestionQueryEngineComplex queries ko sub-questions mein todta haiMulti-doc research
Python — LlamaIndex Basic RAG
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader from llama_index.core import Settings from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI from llama_index.embeddings.openai import OpenAIEmbedding # Settings configure karo (global) Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4o", temperature=0.1) Settings.embed_model = OpenAIEmbedding() # Step 1: Documents load karo documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./my_docs/").load_data() print(f"Loaded {len(documents)} documents") # Step 2: Index banao — automatically chunks & embeds index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents) # Step 3: Query Engine create karo query_engine = index.as_query_engine( similarity_top_k=3 # Top 3 relevant chunks ) # Step 4: Query karo response = query_engine.query("Document mein main topic kya hai?") print(response) print("\n--- Source Nodes ---") for node in response.source_nodes: print(f"Score: {node.score:.2f} | Text: {node.text[:100]}...")
Python — LlamaIndex Agent with Tools
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent from llama_index.core.tools import FunctionTool, QueryEngineTool # === Tool 1: Custom function tool === def multiply_numbers(a: float, b: float) -> float: """Do numbers ko multiply karta hai.""" return a * b math_tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(fn=multiply_numbers) # === Tool 2: Index ko tool banana === doc_tool = QueryEngineTool.from_defaults( query_engine=query_engine, name="document_search", description="Documents mein information search karta hai." ) # === ReAct Agent banao === agent = ReActAgent.from_tools( [math_tool, doc_tool], verbose=True ) # Query karo response = agent.chat("Document mein bataye price ko 1.18 se multiply karo") print(response)
🎤 Interview Questions — LlamaIndex
Q1
LlamaIndex aur LangChain mein kya fundamental difference hai?
Q2
VectorStoreIndex internally kaise kaam karta hai?
Q3
SubQuestionQueryEngine kab use karna chahiye?
Q4
LlamaIndex mein query engine aur chat engine mein kya fark hai?
Framework 4

Chapter 6 — CrewAI 🚢

Intermediate Production Ready
🚢
CrewAI
Role-based multi-agent framework — Agents ki "crew" milkar kaam karti hai

CrewAI ek framework hai jahan aap multiple AI agents ko ek team ki tarah organize kar sakte ho. Har agent ka ek specific role, goal, aur backstory hota hai — jaise ek real company mein CEO, Developer, aur Designer hote hain. Yeh "AI ke workforce" banane ka sabse intuitive framework hai.

CrewAI Core Concepts

🚢 CrewAI Architecture — Team Collaboration
🚢 Crew — Sabhi agents aur tasks ka container
🤵 Agent 1: Researcher
Role: Senior Research Analyst
Goal: Find accurate data
Tools: search_web, read_docs
✍️ Agent 2: Writer
Role: Content Strategist
Goal: Write compelling content
Tools: write_file
🔍 Agent 3: Editor
Role: Quality Reviewer
Goal: Review & improve
Tools: review_content
📋 Tasks (Sequential ya Parallel)
Task 1: Research topic → Researcher
→
Task 2: Write article → Writer
→
Task 3: Review → Editor
Python — CrewAI Complete Example
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI # LLM llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o") search_tool = SerperDevTool() # === Step 1: Agents define karo === researcher = Agent( role="Senior Research Analyst", goal="AI industry ke latest trends dhundho aur accurate data provide karo", backstory="""Tum ek experienced research analyst ho jo 10 saal se technology trends analyze kar raha hai. Tumhara analysis always data-driven aur comprehensive hota hai.""", tools=[search_tool], llm=llm, verbose=True ) writer = Agent( role="Content Strategist", goal="Research ko ek engaging aur educational article mein convert karo", backstory="""Tum ek master storyteller ho jo complex technical topics ko easy language mein explain karna jaante ho.""", llm=llm, verbose=True ) # === Step 2: Tasks define karo === research_task = Task( description="""2024-2025 mein top 5 AI Agent frameworks ke baare mein comprehensive research karo. Include: popularity, use cases, pros/cons.""", expected_output="Detailed research report with data and sources", agent=researcher ) write_task = Task( description="""Research ko use karke ek beginner-friendly blog post likho jo explain kare 'Which AI Framework Should You Choose in 2025?'""", expected_output="800 word blog post in markdown format", agent=writer, context=[research_task] # Research ka output use karega ) # === Step 3: Crew banao === crew = Crew( agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[research_task, write_task], process=Process.sequential, # Ek ke baad ek verbose=True ) # === Step 4: Crew ko kaam pe lagao === result = crew.kickoff() print(result.raw)

CrewAI Process Types

ProcessDescriptionUse Case
Process.sequentialTasks ek ke baad ekResearch → Write → Review pipeline
Process.hierarchicalManager agent tasks assign karta haiComplex projects with delegation
Parallel TasksIndependent tasks simultaneouslyResearch + Design ek saath
💡 CrewAI Best Practice
Agent ka backstory zyada specific rakhne se uska output better hota hai. Vague backstory se agent generic answers deta hai. Real-world professional jaise backstory likho — jaise "Tum IIT passout senior developer ho..."
🎤 Interview Questions — CrewAI
Q1
CrewAI mein Agent, Task, aur Crew ke beech kya relationship hai?
Q2
Sequential vs Hierarchical process mein kya fark hai?
Q3
Task context parameter kab use karte hain?
Q4
CrewAI ko production mein deploy karte waqt kya challenges aate hain?
Framework 5

Chapter 7 — AutoGen ⚡

Intermediate Advanced Production Ready
⚡
AutoGen (Microsoft)
Conversation-driven multi-agent framework by Microsoft Research

AutoGen Microsoft Research ne develop kiya hai. Iska unique approach yeh hai ki sab kuch conversations ke through hota hai — agents ek doosre se baat karke problems solve karte hain. Yeh especially code generation aur execution ke liye bahut powerful hai.

AutoGen ka Conversation Model

⚡ AutoGen — Agent Conversation Flow
👤
UserProxyAgent
User represent karta hai
Code execute karta hai
⇌
🤖
AssistantAgent
LLM-powered
Solutions suggest karta hai
Conversation Example:
User: "Fibonacci series ka code likho"
Assistant: "```python def fib(n)... ```"
User (auto-executes): "Code execute kiya — Output: 0,1,1,2,3..."
Assistant: "✅ Code sahi kaam kar raha hai!"
Python — AutoGen Basic Agent Conversation
import autogen # Configuration config_list = [{ "model": "gpt-4o", "api_key": "your-api-key" }] llm_config = {"config_list": config_list} # === AssistantAgent — LLM powered === assistant = autogen.AssistantAgent( name="AI_Assistant", llm_config=llm_config, system_message="""Tum ek expert Python developer ho. Sada working code do aur explain karo.""" ) # === UserProxyAgent — Code execute karta hai === user_proxy = autogen.UserProxyAgent( name="User_Proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER", # Auto — human se input nahi lega max_consecutive_auto_reply=10, is_termination_msg=lambda x: x.get("content", "").rstrip().endswith("TERMINATE"), code_execution_config={ "work_dir": "coding_workspace", "use_docker": False } ) # === Conversation start karo === user_proxy.initiate_chat( assistant, message=""" Ek Python script banao jo: 1. 1 se 100 tak ke prime numbers find kare 2. Result plot kare matplotlib se 3. CSV mein save kare Reply TERMINATE when done. """ )

AutoGen GroupChat — Multiple Agents

Python — AutoGen GroupChat
import autogen # Multiple agents define karo coder = autogen.AssistantAgent( name="Coder", llm_config=llm_config, system_message="Tum ek expert coder ho. Sirf code likho." ) reviewer = autogen.AssistantAgent( name="Code_Reviewer", llm_config=llm_config, system_message="Tum code review expert ho. Bugs aur improvements batao." ) user_proxy = autogen.UserProxyAgent( name="User", human_input_mode="TERMINATE", code_execution_config={"work_dir": "."} ) # GroupChat setup groupchat = autogen.GroupChat( agents=[user_proxy, coder, reviewer], messages=[], max_round=12 ) manager = autogen.GroupChatManager( groupchat=groupchat, llm_config=llm_config ) # Start karo user_proxy.initiate_chat( manager, message="Ek sorting algorithm implement karo aur review karo" )
🎤 Interview Questions — AutoGen
Q1
AssistantAgent aur UserProxyAgent mein kya fark hai?
Q2
AutoGen mein human_input_mode ke different options kya hain?
Q3
GroupChat aur GroupChatManager kaise kaam karte hain?
Q4
AutoGen ki code execution safety concerns kya hain? Kaise handle karte hain?
Framework 6

Chapter 8 — Semantic Kernel 🔮

Intermediate Advanced Enterprise Grade
🔮
Semantic Kernel (Microsoft)
Enterprise-grade AI SDK — .NET, Python, Java support

Semantic Kernel Microsoft ka enterprise-focused framework hai jo C#/.NET, Python, aur Java mein kaam karta hai. Yeh large enterprises ke liye design kiya gaya hai jahan security, reliability, aur existing systems ke saath integration critical hai. Azure OpenAI ke saath deep integration hai.

Semantic Kernel Unique Concepts

ConceptKya HaiAnalogy
KernelCentral orchestrator — sab kuch yahan se control hota haiCPU of your AI app
PluginsFunctions ka collection — native ya semanticApp ke features
Native FunctionsRegular Python/C# code functionsBuilt-in tools
Semantic FunctionsPrompt templates jo functions ki tarah kaam karte hainAI-powered tools
PlannerAuto-plan karta hai — kaunse functions use kareinAuto-pilot mode
MemoryVector-based semantic memory built-inAI ka RAM
Python — Semantic Kernel Basic Usage
import asyncio import semantic_kernel as sk from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion from semantic_kernel.functions import kernel_function # === Step 1: Kernel create karo === kernel = sk.Kernel() # AI Service add karo kernel.add_service(OpenAIChatCompletion( service_id="openai", ai_model_id="gpt-4o", api_key="your-key" )) # === Step 2: Plugin banao === class MathPlugin: @kernel_function(description="Do numbers add karta hai") def add(self, number1: float, number2: float) -> float: return number1 + number2 @kernel_function(description="Do numbers multiply karta hai") def multiply(self, number1: float, number2: float) -> float: return number1 * number2 # Plugin register karo kernel.add_plugin(MathPlugin(), plugin_name="math") # === Step 3: Semantic Function — prompt as function === summarize_fn = kernel.add_function( function_name="Summarize", plugin_name="TextPlugin", prompt="Yeh text summarize karo 3 points mein:\n{{$input}}" ) # === Step 4: Invoke karo === async def main(): # Native function call math_result = await kernel.invoke( kernel.plugins["math"]["multiply"], number1=15.0, number2=3.0 ) print(f"Math result: {math_result}") # 45.0 # Semantic function call summary = await kernel.invoke( summarize_fn, input="AI Agents complex tasks automate karte hain..." ) print(summary) asyncio.run(main())
📘 Semantic Kernel vs LangChain
Choose Semantic Kernel when:
  • Tumhara organization Microsoft/Azure stack use karta hai
  • C# ya .NET mein development hai
  • Enterprise security aur compliance requirements hain
  • Azure Active Directory integration chahiye
Choose LangChain when:
  • Pure Python development hai
  • Large community support chahiye
  • Rapid prototyping karna hai
🎤 Interview Questions — Semantic Kernel
Q1
Semantic Kernel mein Native Function aur Semantic Function mein kya fark hai?
Q2
Semantic Kernel ke Planner kya hote hain? Auto-planning kaise kaam karta hai?
Q3
Semantic Kernel enterprise settings mein kyu prefer kiya jaata hai?
Framework 7

Chapter 9 — Haystack 🌾

Intermediate Production Ready
🌾
Haystack (deepset)
Production-grade NLP pipelines — Modular, testable, enterprise-ready

Haystack deepset ka framework hai jo NLP pipelines banane ke liye specifically designed hai. Yeh highly modular hai — components ko YAML/JSON se configure kiya jaata hai. Production deployment, testing, aur scalability pe zyada focus hai.

Haystack Core Concepts

🔧
Components
Individual processing units — DocumentStore, Retriever, Reader
🔗
Pipelines
Components ko connect karna — Indexing ya Query pipeline
🗄
DocumentStore
Documents store karna — Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Chroma
🔍
Retriever
Relevant documents dhundhna
🌾 Haystack Pipeline Types
📥 Indexing Pipeline
📄 File Input
↓
✂️ Text Converter
↓
🔢 Embedder
↓
🗄 DocumentStore
🔍 Query Pipeline
❓ User Query
↓
🔢 Query Embedder
↓
🔍 Retriever
↓
🤖 LLM Generator
Python — Haystack RAG Pipeline
from haystack import Pipeline, Document from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryBM25Retriever from haystack.components.builders import PromptBuilder from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator from haystack.document_stores.in_memory import InMemoryDocumentStore # === Step 1: DocumentStore setup === store = InMemoryDocumentStore() store.write_documents([ Document(content="Python ek versatile programming language hai."), Document(content="LangChain LLM applications banane ka framework hai."), Document(content="AI Agents autonomous tasks perform karte hain."), ]) # === Step 2: Prompt Template === template = """ Context: {% for doc in documents %} - {{ doc.content }} {% endfor %} Question: {{question}} Answer: """ # === Step 3: Pipeline banao === pipe = Pipeline() # Components add karo pipe.add_component("retriever", InMemoryBM25Retriever(document_store=store)) pipe.add_component("prompt_builder", PromptBuilder(template=template)) pipe.add_component("llm", OpenAIGenerator()) # Connect karo pipe.connect("retriever", "prompt_builder.documents") pipe.connect("prompt_builder", "llm") # === Step 4: Run karo === result = pipe.run({ "retriever": {"query": "LangChain kya hai?"}, "prompt_builder": {"question": "LangChain kya hai?"} }) print(result["llm"]["replies"][0])
💡 Haystack Production Feature
Haystack pipelines ko YAML files mein serialize kar sakte ho — iska matlab aap code change kiye bina pipeline config change kar sakte ho. Yeh feature DevOps aur CI/CD ke liye bahut useful hai.
Master Comparison

Chapter 10 — Framework Comparison & Decision Guide

Ab sabse important question — "Kaunsa framework use karein?" Yeh ek complete comparison guide hai jo tumhare decision ko easy karega.

Master Comparison Table

Framework Primary Use Case Learning Curve Community Production? Multi-Agent?
🦜 LangChain General LLM apps, RAG, Chains 🟢 Easy ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 90k+ stars ✅ Yes 🟡 Basic
🕸 LangGraph Stateful complex agents, workflows 🟡 Medium ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Growing ✅ Yes ✅ Yes
🦙 LlamaIndex Document intelligence, RAG 🟢 Easy ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 35k+ stars ✅ Yes 🟡 Basic
🚢 CrewAI Role-based multi-agent teams 🟢 Easy ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 20k+ stars ✅ Yes ✅ Yes
⚡ AutoGen Conversational agents, code gen 🟡 Medium ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Microsoft backed ✅ Yes ✅ Yes
🔮 Sem. Kernel Enterprise, .NET/Azure integration 🔴 Hard ⭐⭐⭐ Enterprise ✅ Yes 🟡 Growing
🌾 Haystack NLP pipelines, search systems 🟡 Medium ⭐⭐⭐ Niche but strong ✅ Yes ❌ Limited

Decision Flowchart — Kaunsa Framework Use Karein?

🤔 Framework Selection Decision Tree

Framework Combination Patterns

Real production systems mein aksar multiple frameworks milkaar use hote hain. Yeh common combinations hain:

CombinationUse CaseWhy This Combo?
LangGraph + LlamaIndexResearch assistant agentLangGraph ke graph + LlamaIndex ki document intelligence
CrewAI + LangChainMulti-agent content pipelineCrewAI orchestration + LangChain ke tools
LangGraph + HaystackEnterprise search + workflowHaystack retrieval + LangGraph state management
AutoGen + LlamaIndexCode-aware document agentAutoGen code execution + LlamaIndex doc search
🧠 Remember This — Framework Selection Rule
"Don't fall in love with a framework."
Har problem ke liye best tool choose karo. Sirf LangChain seekhna kaafi nahi — architecture samjho. 2025 mein sabse valuable skill hai: kab kaunsa framework kyu use karna chahiye — yahi tumhe ek average engineer se alag karega.
Advanced

Chapter 11 — Multi-Agent Orchestration

Abhi tak humne individual frameworks dekhe. Ab samjhenge ki multiple agents ek saath kaise milke kaam karte hain — yahi modern AI engineering ka core hai.

Orchestration Patterns

🎭 Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns
1️⃣ Sequential Pipeline
Agent A
→
Agent B
→
Agent C
Output ek ka input doosre ka. Simple, predictable.
2️⃣ Supervisor Pattern
👑 Supervisor
Worker A
Worker B
Worker C
Manager delegates tasks to specialized workers.
3️⃣ Parallel Execution
Input
A
B
C
Aggregator
Saare agents ek saath kaam karte hain.
4️⃣ Debate Pattern
Agent A
⇌
Agent B
Judge Agent
Agents argue, judge decides. Better accuracy.

LangGraph Supervisor Pattern — Code

Python — LangGraph Supervisor Multi-Agent
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated, Literal from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI # State class TeamState(TypedDict): messages: Annotated[list, add_messages] next_agent: str llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o") # Supervisor — decide karta hai kisko kaam dena hai def supervisor_node(state): msgs = state["messages"] system = """Tum supervisor ho. Team: researcher, coder, writer. Decide karo next me kaunsa agent kaam karega. Sirf ek naam do: researcher/coder/writer/FINISH""" response = llm.invoke([{"role": "system", "content": system}] + msgs) return {"next_agent": response.content.strip()} # Worker nodes def researcher_node(state): response = llm.invoke([{"role": "system", "content": "Tum research specialist ho."}] + state["messages"]) return {"messages": [{"role": "assistant", "content": f"[Researcher]: {response.content}"}]} def coder_node(state): response = llm.invoke([{"role": "system", "content": "Tum expert coder ho."}] + state["messages"]) return {"messages": [{"role": "assistant", "content": f"[Coder]: {response.content}"}]} # Routing function def route_to_agent(state) -> Literal["researcher", "coder", "__end__"]: next_a = state.get("next_agent", "") if "FINISH" in next_a: return "__end__" if "researcher" in next_a: return "researcher" return "coder" # Graph build karo graph = StateGraph(TeamState) graph.add_node("supervisor", supervisor_node) graph.add_node("researcher", researcher_node) graph.add_node("coder", coder_node) graph.add_edge(START, "supervisor") graph.add_conditional_edges("supervisor", route_to_agent) graph.add_edge("researcher", "supervisor") graph.add_edge("coder", "supervisor") app = graph.compile() result = app.invoke({ "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "AI agent frameworks research karke Python code likho"}], "next_agent": "" })
⚠️ Multi-Agent Common Mistakes
  • Infinite loops: Termination condition define nahi ki — agents chaklate rahte hain
  • Context overflow: Bahut saari agents ke messages se token limit exceed ho jaati hai
  • Agent hallucinations: Ek agent galat info doosre ko pass karta hai — compounding errors
  • No error handling: Ek agent fail ho toh poora workflow crash ho jaata hai
Revision

Chapter 12 — Glossary & Final Summary

📖 Key Terms Glossary

Agent Framework
AI agents banane ke liye pre-built tools ka collection
LCEL (LangChain Expression Language)
Pipe operator se chains compose karna
State (LangGraph)
Graph execution ke dauran shared data
Checkpoint
State save point — resume karne ke liye
Node (LangGraph)
State process karne wala function
Conditional Edge
Dynamic routing — condition pe based next node
Crew (CrewAI)
Agents + Tasks ka team container
Plugin (Semantic Kernel)
Functions ka named collection
QueryEngine (LlamaIndex)
Documents se structured answers nikalna
GroupChat (AutoGen)
Multiple agents ki conversation group
Pipeline (Haystack)
Connected NLP components ki chain
DocumentStore
Documents store + retrieve karne ka component
Tool Binding
LLM ko tools ke baare mein batana
Human-in-Loop
Agent execution mein human approval step
Orchestration
Multiple agents ko coordinate karna
Supervisor Pattern
Manager agent jo workers ko delegate karta hai
Final Summary
📝 Part 9 Complete Summary — Agent Frameworks
  • Agent Frameworks — ready-made building blocks jo AI agents banane ki process fast karte hain
  • LangChain — sabse popular, general-purpose, LCEL chains + tools + RAG ka go-to framework
  • LangGraph — stateful complex agents ke liye, cyclic graphs, human-in-loop, best for production
  • LlamaIndex — document intelligence aur RAG applications ka specialist framework
  • CrewAI — role-based multi-agent teams, intuitive API, sequential/hierarchical process
  • AutoGen — conversation-driven agents, code execution, Microsoft Research backed
  • Semantic Kernel — enterprise grade, .NET/Azure deep integration, plugins system
  • Haystack — modular NLP pipelines, YAML config, production search systems
  • Multi-Agent Patterns — sequential, supervisor, parallel, debate patterns

Q1

Q2
Ek production AI agent mein aap kaunse frameworks combine karoge aur kyun?
Q3
Multi-agent system mein "supervisor pattern" kaise implement karte hain?
Q4
LangGraph checkpointing ka real use case kya hai? Example do.
Q5
CrewAI vs AutoGen — dono ke pros/cons kya hain? Kab kaunsa better hai?
Q6
Human-in-the-loop agents kab zarooori hain? LangGraph mein kaise implement karte hain?
Q7
Agent ke tools mein docstring kyun important hai?
Q8
LlamaIndex ka SubQuestionQueryEngine kya karta hai aur kab use karein?

  5. Advanced: LangGraph + LlamaIndex combine karke ek research assistant banao
  6. Challenge: AutoGen se ek code review system banao jisme 2 agents hoon — Coder + Reviewer
