📖 Is Chapter Ko Kaise Padhe?
Pehle pura ek chapter padhein, phir code examples manually type karein (copy-paste mat karein!), aur finally mini exercises solve karein. Yeh approach deep understanding deta hai.
Chapter 1 of 6

Backend Deployment

Jab aap ek AI model ya agent banate ho, toh use duniya ke saamne laana padta hai — yani ek server par deploy karna padta hai. Backend deployment matlab hai aapki Python/Node.js application ko ek aise server par chalana jahan se dusre log us API ko access kar sakein.

💡 Analogy — Dukaan Kholna
Socho tumne ek bahut achha khana banana seekh liya. Ab ghar par toh tum khud khate ho — lekin dukaan kholna matlab hai kitchen ko ek aisi jagah banana jahan hazaron log aayen aur order karein. Backend deployment bilkul yahi hai!

1.1 FastAPI — The Modern Python Server

FastAPI Kya Hai?

FastAPI ek modern, high-performance Python web framework hai jo APIs banane ke liye use hota hai. Yeh Starlette (ASGI framework) aur Pydantic (data validation) ke upar build hua hai. Iska naam isliye FastAPI hai kyunki yeh:

  • Fast to run — NodeJS aur Go ke barabar performance
  • Fast to code — 200–300% faster development speed
  • Fast to learn — Minimal boilerplate, maximum clarity

FastAPI Kyun Use Karein?

FeatureFastAPIFlaskDjango
Speed⚡ Very High (async)ModerateModerate
Auto Documentation✅ Built-in Swagger❌ Manual❌ Manual
Type Hints Support✅ Native⚠ Partial⚠ Partial
Async Support✅ First-class⚠ Limited⚠ Limited
Data Validation✅ Pydantic built-in❌ Manual⚠ DRF
Learning CurveEasyEasySteep

FastAPI Kaise Kaam Karta Hai — Internal Working

Client (Browser/App)
↓ HTTP Request
Uvicorn (ASGI Server)
↓ Passes request
Starlette Middleware
↓ Route matching
FastAPI Router
↓ Validates data
Pydantic Models
↓ Runs your code
Your Function (Endpoint)
↑ Returns JSON response
FastAPI Request Processing Pipeline

Installation

bash — Terminal 
# FastAPI aur Uvicorn (ASGI server) install karo
pip install fastapi uvicorn[standard]

# Additional dependencies for AI apps
pip install pydantic python-dotenv httpx

Basic FastAPI App — Beginner Example

Python — main.py 
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

# FastAPI app create karo
app = FastAPI(
    title="AI Agent API",
    version="1.0.0",
    description="Mera pehla AI backend!"
)

# Pydantic model — Request body ka structure
class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    message: str
    user_id: Optional[str] = "anonymous"

# Response model
class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
    reply: str
    tokens_used: int

# GET endpoint — Simple health check
@app.get("/")
async def root():
    return {"status": "✅ Server chal raha hai!"}

# POST endpoint — Chat message receive karo
@app.post("/chat", response_model=ChatResponse)
async def chat(request: ChatRequest):
    # Yahan AI model call hoga (abhi simple response)
    reply = f"Aapne kaha: {request.message}"
    return ChatResponse(
        reply=reply,
        tokens_used=len(request.message.split())
    )

# Run command:
# uvicorn main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

Real-World FastAPI AI App — Claude Integration

Python — ai_server.py 
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel
import anthropic
import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()  # .env file se API keys load karo

app = FastAPI(title="AI Chat Server")

# CORS allow karo (frontend se requests aane ke liye)
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],  # Production mein specific domain dena
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

class Message(BaseModel):
    role: str      # "user" ya "assistant"
    content: str

class ChatReq(BaseModel):
    messages: list[Message]
    system: str = "You are a helpful AI assistant."

@app.post("/api/chat")
async def chat_endpoint(req: ChatReq):
    try:
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(
            api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
        )
        response = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
            max_tokens=1024,
            system=req.system,
            messages=[m.dict() for m in req.messages]
        )
        return {
            "reply": response.content[0].text,
            "usage": response.usage.model_dump()
        }
    except Exception as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
✅ FastAPI Best Practice
Hamesha async def use karo endpoints ke liye jab bhi I/O operations ho (database, external API). Isse server ek request ke wait mein dusre requests serve karta rehta hai.

1.2 Flask — The Microframework

Flask Kya Hai?

Flask ek lightweight Python web framework hai. Yeh "micro" isliye hai kyunki isme bahut kuch built-in nahi hai — tum sirf wahi add karte ho jo chahiye. Flask 2010 mein Armin Ronacher ne banaya tha.

Flask vs FastAPI — Kab Kya Use Karein?

🔵 Flask Use Karo Jab:
  • Simple REST API banana ho
  • Team ko Flask already aata ho
  • ML model ke liye quick server chahiye
  • Chhote projects (startups, prototypes)
🟢 FastAPI Use Karo Jab:
  • High performance chahiye
  • Auto documentation zaroorat ho
  • Async operations heavy ho
  • Production AI APIs
Python — Flask AI Server 
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
from flask_cors import CORS
import anthropic, os

app = Flask(__name__)
CORS(app)  # Cross-origin requests allow karo

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))

@app.route("/health", methods=["GET"])
def health():
    return jsonify({"status": "ok"})

@app.route("/summarize", methods=["POST"])
def summarize():
    data = request.get_json()
    text = data.get("text", "")
    
    if not text:
        return jsonify({"error": "text field chahiye!"}), 400
    
    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",  # Faster, cheaper model
        max_tokens=500,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": f"Yeh text summarize karo:\n\n{text}"
        }]
    )
    return jsonify({"summary": resp.content[0].text})

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=False, host="0.0.0.0", port=5000)

1.3 Node.js — JavaScript Backend

Node.js Kab Use Karein AI Apps Mein?

Node.js tab use karte hain jab:

  • Frontend team JavaScript use karti ho (full-stack JS)
  • Real-time features chahiye ho (WebSockets, Server-Sent Events)
  • Next.js ya Vercel par deploy karna ho
JavaScript — Express.js AI Server 
const express = require('express');
const Anthropic = require('@anthropic-ai/sdk');

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const client = new Anthropic();

// Streaming response — real-time text generate karo
app.post('/stream', async (req, res) => {
    res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream');
    res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache');
    
    const stream = client.messages.stream({
        model: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514',
        max_tokens: 1024,
        messages: [{ role: 'user', content: req.body.message }]
    });
    
    // Har text chunk stream karo client ko
    stream.on('text', (text) => {
        res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({text})}\n\n`);
    });
    
    stream.on('finalMessage', () => {
        res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n');
        res.end();
    });
});

app.listen(3000, () => console.log('🚀 Server port 3000 par chal raha hai!'));

1.4 Serverless Architecture

Serverless Kya Hai?

Serverless matlab yeh nahi ki server nahi hai — matlab yeh hai ki aapko server manage nahi karna padta. Cloud provider automatically:

  • Server start karta hai jab request aati hai
  • Request handle karta hai
  • Server band kar deta hai baad mein
  • Sirf actual use ka bill karta hai
Traditional Server vs Serverless Comparison

Serverless Platforms

⚡
AWS Lambda
Most popular, Python/Node support
▲
Vercel Functions
Best for Next.js apps
🌐
Cloudflare Workers
Edge computing, ultra-fast
🔥
Google Cloud Functions
GCP ecosystem integration
Python — AWS Lambda Function (AI) 
import json
import anthropic
import os

# Lambda handler function — yeh AWS call karta hai
def handler(event, context):
    """
    event: HTTP request data (body, headers, etc.)
    context: Lambda runtime information
    """
    try:
        # Request body parse karo
        body = json.loads(event.get('body', '{}'))
        message = body.get('message', '')
        
        if not message:
            return {
                'statusCode': 400,
                'body': json.dumps({'error': 'message required'})
            }
        
        # Claude API call
        client = anthropic.Anthropic(
            api_key=os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']  # Env var se lo
        )
        resp = client.messages.create(
            model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
            max_tokens=256,
            messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': message}]
        )
        
        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
            'body': json.dumps({
                'reply': resp.content[0].text
            })
        }
    except Exception as e:
        return {'statusCode': 500, 'body': json.dumps({'error': str(e)})}
⚠ Serverless Cold Start Problem
Jab Lambda function pehli baar ya kaafi time baad call ho, toh startup mein 1-3 seconds lag sakte hain. Yeh "cold start" problem AI apps mein annoying ho sakta hai. Solution: Provisioned Concurrency (AWS) ya always-on instances use karo.
  • Code likhao — FastAPI/Flask/Lambda function
  • Dependencies bundle karo — requirements.txt + pip install
  • Environment variables set karo — API keys securely
  • Deploy karo — Cloud provider par upload
  • Test karo — Postman se API test
  • Monitor karo — Logs aur errors track
💬 Interview Questions — Backend Deployment
  • FastAPI aur Flask mein kya main difference hai? Kab kaunsa use karein?
  • ASGI aur WSGI mein kya fark hai? FastAPI kaunsa use karta hai aur kyun?
  • Serverless ka cold start problem kya hai aur ise kaise solve karein?
  • FastAPI mein Pydantic models ka kya role hai?
  • Production mein Flask/FastAPI ko Gunicorn ke saath kyun run karte hain?
🏋 Mini Exercise — Backend Deployment
  1. FastAPI install karo aur ek simple /hello endpoint banao jo JSON return kare
  2. Ek /summarize POST endpoint banao jo text input le aur 3 bullet points return kare
  3. Swagger UI dekho at http://localhost:8000/docs
  4. Flask mein same API banao aur dono ka code compare karo
Chapter 2 of 6

Cloud Platforms

Cloud platform ek remote computer infrastructure hai jisme aap apni application deploy karte ho. Ghar ke computer par nahi — Amazon, Google, Microsoft ke data centers mein! Yeh companies aapko on-demand computing resources provide karti hain.

💡 Cloud Ka Ek Simple Analogy
Socho electricity. Aap ghar mein generator nahi lagaate — simply power company se bijli khareedtay ho. Usi tarah cloud computing mein aap servers nahi khareedtay — simply computing power rent karte ho!

Cloud Service Models — IaaS, PaaS, SaaS

Cloud Service Models: IaaS vs PaaS vs SaaS

2.1 Amazon Web Services (AWS)

AWS duniya ka sabse bada cloud provider hai — market share ~32%! 200+ services offer karta hai. AI applications ke liye AWS ke key services:

AWS ServiceKya Karta HaiAI App Use Case
EC2Virtual Machines (Servers)FastAPI/Flask server run karna
LambdaServerless FunctionsAI endpoints, webhooks
S3Object StorageDocuments, images, model files store karna
RDSManaged SQL DatabaseUser data, conversation history
DynamoDBNoSQL DatabaseSession data, fast key-value lookups
ElastiCacheRedis/MemcachedResponse caching, rate limiting
API GatewayAPI ManagementLambda ke aage reverse proxy
ECS/EKSContainer OrchestrationDocker containers run karna
CloudWatchMonitoring & LogsApp errors aur metrics track karna
BedrockManaged AI ModelsClaude, Llama APIs access karna

AWS Deployment Flow — FastAPI on EC2

bash — EC2 Deployment Steps 
# Step 1: EC2 instance mein SSH karo
ssh -i "your-key.pem" ubuntu@your-ec2-public-ip

# Step 2: Python aur dependencies install karo
sudo apt update && sudo apt install python3-pip nginx -y

# Step 3: Apna code clone/copy karo
git clone https://github.com/your-repo/ai-app.git
cd ai-app
pip3 install -r requirements.txt

# Step 4: Environment variables set karo
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."

# Step 5: Gunicorn se run karo (production WSGI server)
gunicorn -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app \
  --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 \
  --daemon  # background mein run karo

# Step 6: Nginx as reverse proxy configure karo
# /etc/nginx/sites-available/ai-app mein:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name your-domain.com;
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}

2.2 Microsoft Azure

Azure Microsoft ka cloud platform hai — enterprises aur government sector mein bahut popular. AI ke liye Azure OpenAI Service aur Azure AI Studio famous hain.

Key Azure Services for AI

🤖
Azure OpenAI
GPT-4, DALL-E access with enterprise security
🔍
Azure AI Search
Vector search for RAG applications
⚡
Azure Functions
Serverless compute (like AWS Lambda)
📦
Azure Container Apps
Docker containers easily deploy karo
🧠
Azure ML Studio
ML model training aur deployment
🗄
Cosmos DB
Global NoSQL database

2.3 Google Cloud Platform (GCP)

GCP Google ka cloud hai — AI/ML ke liye sabse advanced platform mana jata hai kyunki Google ne hi TensorFlow, Kubernetes, aur Transformer architecture create kiya.

GCP AI-Specific Services

ServiceKya Hai
Vertex AIComplete ML platform — train, deploy, monitor models
Gemini APIGoogle ka LLM family access karo
Cloud RunContainerized apps serverless run karo
BigQueryPetabyte-scale data warehouse + ML
TPU (v5)Tensor Processing Units — ML training ke liye
FirebaseRealtime database + hosting for web/mobile AI apps

Cloud Run Deployment — Easiest Production Deploy

bash — Google Cloud Run Deploy 
# Step 1: Google Cloud SDK install aur login
gcloud auth login
gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID

# Step 2: Docker image build aur push karo
gcloud builds submit --tag gcr.io/YOUR_PROJECT/ai-app

# Step 3: Cloud Run par deploy karo — bas ek command!
gcloud run deploy ai-app \
  --image gcr.io/YOUR_PROJECT/ai-app \
  --platform managed \
  --region asia-south1 \
  --allow-unauthenticated \
  --set-env-vars ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..." \
  --memory 512Mi \
  --cpu 1

# Output: https://ai-app-xxx-uc.a.run.app
# Bas! Aapki app live hai! 🎉
🧠 Remember This — Cloud Selection Guide
  • AWS choose karo → Agar team AWS experienced hai ya most popular option chahiye
  • GCP choose karo → Agar AI/ML heavy workload hai ya Vertex AI use karna hai
  • Azure choose karo → Agar enterprise/government client hai ya Microsoft ecosystem mein ho
  • Render/Railway choose karo → Side projects aur quick demos ke liye (cheapest!)
💬 Interview Questions — Cloud Platforms
  • AWS EC2 aur Lambda mein kya fundamental difference hai?
  • IaaS, PaaS, SaaS explain karo real-world examples ke saath
  • Cloud Run aur Kubernetes Engine (GKE) mein kab kya prefer karein?
  • Multi-region deployment kyun zaroori hai production AI apps ke liye?
  • AWS S3 ko AI applications mein kaise use karte hain?
Chapter 3 of 6

DevOps — Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD

DevOps = Development + Operations. Yeh ek culture aur set of practices hai jo software development aur IT operations ko combine karta hai taaki applications faster, more reliably, aur at scale deliver ho sakein.

💡 DevOps Ka Essence
Pehle developers code likhte the aur "wall ke upar phenk dete the" — Operations team handle karti thi. DevOps mein dono collaborate karte hain: code likhna, test karna, aur deploy karna — sab ek seamless pipeline mein!

3.1 Docker — Containerization

Docker Kya Hai?

Docker ek tool hai jo application aur uski sari dependencies ko ek portable "container" mein pack karta hai. Container ek chhota, isolated environment hota hai jisme aapka app run hota hai.

The Classic Problem — "Works on My Machine!"

The "Works on My Machine" Problem — Docker ise solve karta hai

Docker Solution — Container = App + Everything It Needs

Docker Container — Ek Jagah Pack, Kahin Bhi Run

Dockerfile — Recipe for Container

Dockerfile — AI FastAPI App 
# Base image choose karo — Python 3.11 slim version
FROM python:3.11-slim

# Container ke andar working directory set karo
WORKDIR /app

# Pehle requirements copy karo (caching ke liye)
COPY requirements.txt .

# Dependencies install karo
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Baaki code copy karo
COPY . .

# Port 8000 expose karo (container ke bahar dikhne ke liye)
EXPOSE 8000

# Container start hone par yeh command run hogi
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

docker-compose.yml — Multiple Services Chalao

YAML — docker-compose.yml 
# docker-compose up -d se sab kuch start hoga
version: '3.8'

services:
  # FastAPI AI Application
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
    depends_on:
      - redis
      - postgres
    restart: unless-stopped

  # Redis — Caching ke liye
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"

  # PostgreSQL — Database
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: aiapp
      POSTGRES_USER: admin
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

volumes:
  pgdata:

3.2 Kubernetes (K8s) — Container Orchestration

Kubernetes Kya Hai?

Kubernetes ek container orchestration platform hai jo automatically:

  • Deploy karta hai containers ko multiple servers par
  • Scale karta hai based on traffic
  • Heal karta hai — crashed containers restart karta hai
  • Load balance karta hai requests ke beech
Kubernetes Architecture — Control Plane + Worker Nodes

K8s Deployment YAML

YAML — Kubernetes Deployment 
# deployment.yaml — AI App ko K8s par deploy karo
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ai-api-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3   # 3 copies chalao simultaneously
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ai-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ai-api
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: ai-api
          image: gcr.io/my-project/ai-app:v1.2
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
          env:
            - name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:   # Secret se lo, hardcode mat karo!
                  name: api-secrets
                  key: anthropic-key
          resources:
            requests: { memory: "256Mi", cpu: "250m" }
            limits: { memory: "512Mi", cpu: "500m" }
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ai-api-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: ai-api
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8000
  type: LoadBalancer  # Automatically traffic distribute karo

3.3 CI/CD — Continuous Integration & Deployment

CI/CD Kya Hai?

CI (Continuous Integration) = Code push hone par automatically tests run karo.
CD (Continuous Deployment) = Tests pass hone par automatically deploy karo.

Code Push
→
Tests Run
→
Build Image
→
Staging Deploy
→
Production Deploy
CI/CD Pipeline — Code se Production tak Automated Journey

3.4 GitHub Actions

GitHub Actions ek free CI/CD platform hai jo GitHub repository ke saath built-in aata hai. YAML files likhke automation define karte hain.

YAML — .github/workflows/deploy.yml 
name: AI App CI/CD Pipeline

# Trigger: main branch par push hone par
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  # Job 1: Tests chalao
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Code checkout karo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Python setup
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Dependencies install karo
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Tests run karo
        run: pytest tests/ -v
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

  # Job 2: Deploy (sirf test pass hone par)
  deploy:
    needs: test   # test job ke baad chalega
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Google Cloud auth
        uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
        with:
          credentials_json: ${{ secrets.GCP_CREDENTIALS }}

      - name: Docker image build & push
        run: |
          gcloud builds submit --tag gcr.io/${{ env.PROJECT_ID }}/ai-app:${{ github.sha }}

      - name: Cloud Run deploy
        run: |
          gcloud run deploy ai-app \
            --image gcr.io/${{ env.PROJECT_ID }}/ai-app:${{ github.sha }} \
            --region asia-south1 \
            --platform managed
✅ GitHub Actions Best Practice
Hamesha API keys aur secrets ko GitHub Secrets mein store karo — never hardcode karo YAML mein. Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions mein jaake add karo.
💬 Interview Questions — DevOps
  • Docker image aur Docker container mein kya difference hai?
  • Kubernetes mein Pod, Deployment, aur Service ka kya role hai?
  • CI/CD pipeline kaise AI application development speed badhata hai?
  • docker-compose vs Kubernetes — kab kaunsa use karein?
  • GitHub Actions mein secrets kaise securely manage karte hain?
🏋 Mini Exercise — DevOps
  1. FastAPI app ke liye Dockerfile likhao aur build karo: docker build -t my-ai-app .
  2. Container run karo: docker run -p 8000:8000 -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=xxx my-ai-app
  3. GitHub Actions workflow banao jo tests run kare har PR par
  4. docker-compose.yml likho jisme API + Redis dono ho
Chapter 4 of 6

Scalability — Scale Karna Seekho

Scalability ek system ki ability hai ki jab load badhta hai toh usse handle kar sake — performance degrade hue bina. AI applications ke liye especially important hai kyunki LLM calls expensive aur slow hote hain.

💡 Scalability Analogy — Dhaba vs Restaurant Chain
Ek dhabe mein ek hi cook sab kuch karta hai — 100 log aaye toh problem! Lekin McDonald's ek system hai: multiple stations, multiple workers, standardized process. Scalability ka matlab hai apni app ko McDonald's jaisa banana!

4.1 Load Balancing

Load Balancer Kya Hai?

Load Balancer ek traffic cop hai jo incoming requests ko multiple servers mein distribute karta hai taaki koi ek server overloaded na ho.

Load Balancer — Traffic Distribute Karna

Load Balancing Algorithms

AlgorithmKaise Kaam Karta HaiBest For
Round RobinServer 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3... (rotate karo)Equal servers, simple cases
Least ConnectionsSabse kam busy server ko bhejoVariable processing time (AI calls!)
IP HashUser ka IP dekh ke same server pe bhejoSession persistence (login apps)
WeightedPowerful servers ko zyada trafficMixed hardware setups
RandomRandom server choose karoSimple stateless services
✅ AI App ke Liye Best Algorithm
AI apps ke liye Least Connections use karo. Kyunki Claude/GPT calls ka response time variable hota hai — koi query 2 sec mein done, koi 10 sec mein. Least connections algorithm smartly route karta hai.

4.2 Caching — Repeat Kaam Mat Karo

Caching Kya Hai?

Caching matlab hai pehle se compute kiye results store karna taaki baar baar wahi kaam na karna pade. AI apps mein yeh bahut important hai kyunki LLM calls expensive ($) aur slow (seconds) hote hain.

Python — Redis Caching with FastAPI 
import redis
import hashlib
import json
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()
cache = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379)

def get_cache_key(text: str) -> str:
    # Text ka unique hash banao (same text = same key)
    return f"ai:response:{hashlib.md5(text.encode()).hexdigest()}"

@app.post("/summarize")
async def summarize(text: str):
    key = get_cache_key(text)
    
    # Step 1: Cache check karo pehle
    cached = cache.get(key)
    if cached:
        return {"summary": json.loads(cached), "from_cache": True}
    
    # Step 2: Cache miss — AI call karo
    result = await call_claude_api(text)
    
    # Step 3: Result cache mein save karo (1 hour ke liye)
    cache.setex(key, 3600, json.dumps(result))
    
    return {"summary": result, "from_cache": False}
    
# Cache hit rate 70%+ → Cost 70% kam! 🎉

Caching Strategies

📥
Cache-Aside
App manually cache check karta hai (most common)
✍
Write-Through
DB write ke saath hi cache update karo
🔄
Write-Behind
Pehle cache likhao, baad mein DB sync karo
⏰
TTL-Based
Time limit ke baad cache expire hota hai

4.3 Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling

⬆ Vertical Scaling (Scale Up)

Ek hi server ko powerful banana — zyada RAM, zyada CPU, zyada storage.

  • ✅ Simple, no code changes
  • ❌ Expensive, limit hoti hai
  • ❌ Single point of failure

Example: 4 GB RAM → 32 GB RAM server

➡ Horizontal Scaling (Scale Out)

Zyada servers add karna — load distribute karo multiple machines mein.

  • ✅ Theoretically unlimited scale
  • ✅ No single point of failure
  • ❌ Complex, load balancer chahiye

Example: 1 server → 10 servers

4.4 Queue Systems — Async Processing

Queue Kya Hai?

Queue ek waiting line hai. Jab AI task expensive ho (video generate, report create, bulk emails) toh user ko wait karwane ki jagah task queue mein daalo — background mein process karo — user ko notification do.

User Request
→
API Server
(Instantly returns job_id)
→
📬 Message Queue
(Redis/RabbitMQ/SQS)
→
Worker Process
(AI kaam karta hai)
→
Result Store
+ User Notify
Async Queue Pattern — AI Heavy Tasks ke Liye Ideal
Python — Celery + Redis Queue 
# tasks.py — Background AI tasks
from celery import Celery
import anthropic

# Redis ko message broker use karo
celery_app = Celery(
    'tasks',
    broker='redis://localhost:6379/0',
    backend='redis://localhost:6379/1'
)

@celery_app.task
def analyze_document(doc_text: str, user_id: str):
    """Ye background mein chalega — user ko wait nahi karna"""
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    result = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        max_tokens=2000,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": f"Yeh document deeply analyze karo:\n{doc_text}"
        }]
    )
    # Email/notification bhejo user ko
    send_email(user_id, result.content[0].text)
    return result.content[0].text

# main.py mein FastAPI endpoint
@app.post("/analyze")
async def start_analysis(doc: str, user_id: str):
    # Task queue mein daalo, instantly return karo
    task = analyze_document.delay(doc, user_id)
    return {"job_id": task.id, "status": "queued"}
    # User turant job_id milta hai — wait nahi!
💬 Interview Questions — Scalability
  • Horizontal aur vertical scaling mein kya difference hai? AI apps ke liye kaunsa better hai?
  • Cache invalidation kya hai aur yeh kyun challenging hai?
  • Message queue kab use karte hain API response ke bajaaye?
  • Load balancer kaise decide karta hai ki kaunsa server request handle karega?
  • Redis aur Memcached mein kya difference hai?
Chapter 5 of 6

Security — Apni AI App Ko Safe Banao

Security ek afterthought nahi — architecture ka core part hai. AI applications especially vulnerable hote hain kyunki:

  • Sensitive user data handle karte hain
  • Expensive API calls vulnerable to abuse
  • Prompt injection attacks possible hain
  • LLM outputs unpredictable ho sakte hain

5.1 API Security

Authentication vs Authorization

🔑 Authentication (AuthN)

"Tum kaun ho?" — Identity verify karna

  • Username + Password
  • API Keys
  • OAuth tokens
  • Biometrics
🛡 Authorization (AuthZ)

"Tumhara haq kya hai?" — Permission check karna

  • User: read only
  • Admin: read + write
  • Super Admin: everything
  • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

JWT — JSON Web Tokens

JWT ek self-contained token hai jisme user ki information encoded hoti hai. Server ko database check nahi karna parta — token khud proof hai!

JWT Structure — Header.Payload.Signature
Python — FastAPI JWT Authentication 
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
import jwt
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import os

SECRET_KEY = os.getenv("JWT_SECRET")  # Strong secret key chahiye!
security = HTTPBearer()

def create_token(user_id: str) -> str:
    payload = {
        "user_id": user_id,
        "exp": datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=24)  # 24h expiry
    }
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET_KEY, algorithm="HS256")

def verify_token(creds: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security)):
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(creds.credentials, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=["HS256"])
        return payload
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        raise HTTPException(401, "Token expired! Dobara login karo.")
    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
        raise HTTPException(401, "Invalid token!")

# Protected endpoint — sirf authenticated users
@app.post("/api/chat")
async def chat(message: str, user = Depends(verify_token)):
    # user["user_id"] se pata chalta hai kaun hai
    return {"reply": f"Hello {user['user_id']}!"}

5.2 Rate Limiting — Abuse Rokna

Rate limiting matlab ek user kitni requests per minute kar sakta hai — yeh limit set karna. AI apps ke liye critical hai kyunki bina limit ke koi ek user thousands of dollars ka API cost kar sakta hai!

Python — Rate Limiting with Redis 
import redis
from fastapi import HTTPException, Request
from functools import wraps

redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost')

def rate_limit(max_requests: int = 10, window_seconds: int = 60):
    """10 requests per 60 seconds per user"""
    def decorator(func):
        @wraps(func)
        async def wrapper(request: Request, *args, **kwargs):
            # User ka IP ya user_id se key banao
            client_ip = request.client.host
            key = f"rate:{client_ip}"
            
            # Current count check karo
            count = redis_client.get(key)
            if count and int(count) >= max_requests:
                raise HTTPException(
                    429,
                    f"Bahut zyada requests! {window_seconds}s baad try karo."
                )
            
            # Count increment karo
            redis_client.incr(key)
            redis_client.expire(key, window_seconds)  # Auto expire
            
            return await func(request, *args, **kwargs)
        return wrapper
    return decorator

# Use karna aasaan hai:
@app.post("/chat")
@rate_limit(max_requests=10, window_seconds=60)
async def chat(request: Request, ...):
    ...

5.3 Secret Management — Keys Safely Store Karna

🚨 NEVER DO THIS — Critical Security Mistake
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = "sk-ant-api03-abc123..."

API key kabhi bhi code mein hardcode mat karo! GitHub par push ho gayi toh 5 minutes mein koi misuse kar sakta hai. Yeh real incident bahut baar hua hai — millions ka bill aa gaya!

Sahi Tarika — Environment Variables & Secrets Managers

bash — Secure Secret Management 
# Method 1: .env file (local development)
# .env file banao (NEVER commit to git!)
echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-..." > .env
echo ".env" >> .gitignore  # Zaroori!

# Python mein use karo:
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()
key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")

# Method 2: AWS Secrets Manager (production)
import boto3
import json

def get_secret(secret_name):
    client = boto3.client('secretsmanager', region_name='ap-south-1')
    response = client.get_secret_value(SecretId=secret_name)
    return json.loads(response['SecretString'])

secrets = get_secret("ai-app/production")
api_key = secrets["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"]

# Method 3: Kubernetes Secrets
kubectl create secret generic api-secrets \
  --from-literal=anthropic-key="sk-ant-..."
  # YAML mein reference karo (Ch.3 deployment.yaml dekho)

5.4 HTTPS & Encryption

Security LayerKya Protect Karta HaiImplementation
HTTPS/TLSTransit mein data encryptionLet's Encrypt (free SSL), Cloudflare
At-Rest EncryptionDatabase mein stored dataAWS RDS encryption, PostgreSQL pgcrypto
Input ValidationSQL injection, XSS attacksPydantic models, parameterized queries
CORS PolicyUnauthorized domains se requestsFastAPI CORSMiddleware with whitelist
API Key RotationLeaked key damage minimizeMonthly rotation, monitoring alerts

5.5 Prompt Injection Security — AI-Specific

Python — Prompt Injection Defense 
import re

def sanitize_user_input(user_message: str) -> str:
    """
    Prompt injection attacks filter karo:
    - "Ignore all instructions" jaisi strings
    - System prompt override attempts
    - Jailbreak patterns
    """
    # Common injection patterns
    injection_patterns = [
        r"ignore (all |previous |above )?instructions",
        r"you are now",
        r"forget (everything|all)",
        r"new (persona|role|identity)",
        r"\[SYSTEM\]",
        r"###\s*instruction",
    ]
    
    clean_msg = user_message.lower()
    for pattern in injection_patterns:
        if re.search(pattern, clean_msg, re.IGNORECASE):
            raise ValueError("Suspicious input detected! Allowed nahi hai.")
    
    # Length limit bhi lagao
    if len(user_message) > 10000:
        raise ValueError("Input too long!")
    
    return user_message.strip()
💬 Interview Questions — Security
  • Authentication aur Authorization mein kya difference hai? Example do.
  • JWT ka structure kya hai? Signature kyun zaroori hai?
  • Rate limiting kaise implement karein Redis ke saath?
  • API keys securely manage karne ke best practices kya hain?
  • Prompt injection attack kya hai? AI apps mein kaise defend karein?
  • HTTPS aur HTTP mein kya fark hai? SSL/TLS kaise kaam karta hai?
Chapter 6 of 6

Cost Optimization — Paisa Bachao, Performance Rakho

AI applications ke saath sabse bada challenge hai cost. LLM APIs expensive hote hain — agar smart nahi rahe toh monthly bill lakhs mein aa sakta hai. Yeh chapter aapko production mein cost significantly kam karna sikhata hai bina performance sacrifice kiye.

⚠ Real Cost Warning
GPT-4 ya Claude Sonnet ke saath ek popular app jo sirf 100 users process karta hai per day — without optimization — easily $500–2000/month cost kar sakta hai. With proper optimization, yeh $50–200 ho sakta hai!

6.1 Model Routing — Sahi Kaam Ke Liye Sahi Model

Model Routing Kya Hai?

Har query ke liye sabse expensive model use karna jaroori nahi! Model routing matlab hai task ki complexity ke hisaab se sahi model choose karna.

Model Routing — Task Complexity Ke Hisaab Se Model Choose Karo
Python — Smart Model Router 
import anthropic

def route_to_model(query: str) -> str:
    """Query ki complexity dekh ke best model choose karo"""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    word_count = len(query.split())
    
    # Simple queries → Haiku (cheapest, fastest)
    simple_keywords = ["kya hai", "what is", "define", "translate"]
    if word_count < 20 or any(k in query_lower for k in simple_keywords):
        return "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"  # 60x cheaper!
    
    # Complex queries → Opus (expensive but best)
    complex_keywords = ["analyze", "research", "strategy", "compare"]
    if word_count > 100 or any(k in query_lower for k in complex_keywords):
        return "claude-opus-4-6"
    
    # Default → Sonnet (balanced)
    return "claude-sonnet-4-6"

async def smart_chat(user_query: str):
    model = route_to_model(user_query)
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    
    response = client.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_query}]
    )
    return {"reply": response.content[0].text, "model_used": model}

6.2 Prompt Optimization — Token Waste Rokna

Tokens Kya Hain?

LLMs text ko "tokens" mein process karte hain — roughly 1 token ≈ 0.75 English words. Longer prompts = more tokens = more cost. Har word counts!

TechniqueExampleToken Saving
Concise System Prompt"Helpful AI" vs 500-word description~80% system prompt tokens
Remove Redundancy"Please kindly summarize" → "Summarize"30-40% reduction
Structured FormatJSON output instead of verbose text50-60% output tokens
Chunking Long DocsSplit 100-page PDF into sectionsOnly relevant chunks send
Caching (Prompt Cache)Anthropic's cache feature for long system promptsUp to 90% on system prompt

Anthropic Prompt Caching — Game Changer

Python — Prompt Caching (Saves 90% on Long System Prompts) 
import anthropic

# Lambi system prompt ek baar cache karo
long_system_prompt = """
Tum ek expert financial advisor ho jo Indian markets mein specialize 
karta hai. Tumhare paas 20 saal ka experience hai aur tum SEBI 
certified ho. ... [10,000 characters ka detailed prompt]
"""

def chat_with_caching(user_message: str):
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    
    return client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=1024,
        system=[
            {
                "type": "text",
                "text": long_system_prompt,
                "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}  # ← Magic!
            }
        ],
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}]
    )
    # Pehli call: full cost
    # Subsequent calls: 90% cheaper! Cache hit = 0.1x cost

6.3 Batch Inference

Batch processing matlab hai ek saath many requests ek batch mein process karna. Real-time nahi chahiye? Batch use karo — 50% discount milta hai Anthropic par!

Python — Anthropic Batch API (50% Cost Saving!) 
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# 100 documents batch mein process karo
batch_requests = [
    {
        "custom_id": f"doc_{i}",
        "params": {
            "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            "max_tokens": 200,
            "messages": [{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Summarize: {docs[i]}"
            }]
        }
    }
    for i in range(len(docs))
]

# Batch submit karo
batch = client.messages.batches.create(requests=batch_requests)
print(f"Batch ID: {batch.id}")

# Results fetch karo jab ready ho (24 hours tak)
for result in client.messages.batches.results(batch.id):
    if result.result.type == "succeeded":
        print(result.result.message.content[0].text)

6.4 GPU Optimization — Self-Hosted Models

Kab Self-Host Karna Chahiye?

Agar aap bahut zyada requests kar rahe ho, toh open-source models (Llama, Mistral) GPU par self-host karna cheaper pad sakta hai long term mein.

ScaleBest OptionMonthly Cost (est.)
< 1M tokens/monthAPI (Claude/GPT)$5–50
1M–50M tokens/monthAPI + Caching + Routing$50–500
50M–500M tokens/monthHybrid (API + Self-hosted)$500–2000
> 500M tokens/monthSelf-hosted Llama/Mistral on GPU$2000 flat (GPU server)
bash — Ollama — Local LLM Server (Free!) 
# Ollama install karo — local LLM server
curl -fsSL https://ollama.ai/install.sh | sh

# Llama 3.1 8B model download karo (4.7 GB)
ollama pull llama3.1

# Server start karo (OpenAI-compatible API!)
ollama serve

# Python se use karo — same code, different endpoint
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    base_url='http://localhost:11434/v1',  # Local server!
    api_key='ollama'  # Key ki zaroorat nahi
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='llama3.1',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello!'}]
)
# 100% FREE! No API cost. But GPU chahiye best performance ke liye.

6.5 Quantization — Model Size Compress Karna

Quantization matlab model ke weights ko lower precision format mein convert karna (float32 → int8/int4). Isse:

  • Memory 4x–8x kam lagti hai
  • Speed 2x–4x fast hoti hai
  • Quality thodi si decrease hoti hai (5% tak)
Python — Quantized Model with Transformers 
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, BitsAndBytesConfig
import torch

# 4-bit quantization config
quantization_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
    load_in_4bit=True,       # 4-bit precision use karo
    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.float16,
    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,  # Double quantization
)

# Llama 7B: normally 14GB GPU → 4-bit = 4GB GPU!
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
    "meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf",
    quantization_config=quantization_config,
    device_map="auto"
)
print("Model loaded! Memory: ~4GB instead of 14GB 🎉")

6.6 Cost Monitoring — Paisa Track Karo

Python — Token Usage Tracking 
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
import json

# Claude Sonnet pricing (approximate)
PRICE_PER_MILLION_INPUT = 3.0   # $3 per 1M input tokens
PRICE_PER_MILLION_OUTPUT = 15.0  # $15 per 1M output tokens

def track_usage(response, user_id: str):
    usage = response.usage
    
    # Cost calculate karo
    input_cost = (usage.input_tokens / 1_000_000) * PRICE_PER_MILLION_INPUT
    output_cost = (usage.output_tokens / 1_000_000) * PRICE_PER_MILLION_OUTPUT
    total_cost = input_cost + output_cost
    
    # Log karo database/file mein
    log_entry = {
        "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        "user_id": user_id,
        "input_tokens": usage.input_tokens,
        "output_tokens": usage.output_tokens,
        "cost_usd": round(total_cost, 6)
    }
    save_to_db(log_entry)
    
    # Alert if user zyada kharch kar raha hai
    if total_cost > 0.10:  # 10 cents per request limit
        alert_admin(f"High cost alert: {user_id} = ${total_cost:.4f}")
    
    return total_cost
Model Routing

Sahi model use karo — 60x cost difference hai Haiku aur Opus mein

Caching

Same queries dobara process mat karo — 70%+ cost bachate hain popular apps

Batch Processing

Real-time zaroorat nahi? Batch API use karo — 50% discount!

Prompt Optimization

Concise prompts likhna + Prompt Caching = dramatic cost reduction

Monitoring

Hamesha usage track karo — surprises nahi aayenge month end par

Quantization

Self-hosted models 4-bit quantize karo — 4x memory savings

💬 Interview Questions — Cost Optimization
  • Model routing kya hai? Production mein kaise implement karein?
  • Semantic caching aur exact-match caching mein kya fark hai?
  • Anthropic prompt caching kaise kaam karta hai? Kab use karein?
  • Quantization kya hai? INT4 quantization mein kya tradeoffs hain?
  • AI application ke monthly cost ko 80% kaise reduce karein?
🏋 Mini Exercise — Cost Optimization
  1. Ek simple model router likhao jo query length ke basis par Haiku ya Sonnet choose kare
  2. Redis caching setup karo aur same query ke response time compare karo (cache hit vs miss)
  3. Anthropic API call ke baad token usage log karo aur estimated cost calculate karo
  4. Ollama install karo aur locally Llama model run karo — API cost = $0!

📖 Glossary — Key Terms

FastAPI
Modern Python web framework for building APIs — fast, async, auto-docs
ASGI
Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface — FastAPI ka foundation
Serverless
Server manage kiye bina code run karna — pay per execution
Cold Start
Serverless function ka pehli baar ya idle state ke baad slow startup
Docker
Application ko portable container mein pack karna — "runs everywhere"
Container
Isolated environment jisme app + all dependencies packed hoti hain
Kubernetes (K8s)
Container orchestration platform — auto-deploy, scale, heal karta hai
Pod
Kubernetes ka smallest deployable unit — ek ya zyada containers
CI/CD
Continuous Integration/Deployment — automatic test aur deploy pipeline
Load Balancer
Traffic ko multiple servers mein distribute karne wala component
Redis
In-memory data store — caching aur session management ke liye
Message Queue
Async tasks ke liye waiting line — Celery/RabbitMQ/SQS
JWT
JSON Web Token — self-contained authentication token
Rate Limiting
Ek user ki requests per time unit limit karna
Prompt Caching
Long prompts ko Anthropic servers par cache karna — 90% savings
Model Routing
Task complexity ke basis par sahi LLM choose karna
Quantization
Model weights ko lower precision mein compress karna — 4x memory savings
Horizontal Scaling
Zyada servers add karna traffic handle karne ke liye
IaaS/PaaS/SaaS
Cloud service models — Infrastructure/Platform/Software as a Service
Gunicorn
Production-grade Python WSGI server — multiple workers run karta hai

📝 Revision Notes — Part 13 Summary

🧠 Chapter 1 — Backend Deployment
  • FastAPI = Modern async Python server, auto-docs, Pydantic validation
  • Flask = Lightweight, good for simple APIs aur ML model serving
  • Node.js = Best for streaming responses aur real-time apps
  • Serverless = Pay per request, auto scale, cold start problem hoti hai
🧠 Chapter 2 — Cloud Platforms
  • IaaS = Aap OS manage karo (EC2), PaaS = Sirf code do (Cloud Run), SaaS = Bas use karo (Gmail)
  • AWS = Most popular, 200+ services, EC2/Lambda/S3/RDS
  • GCP = AI/ML best (Vertex AI, TPUs), Cloud Run = easiest deploy
  • Azure = Enterprise-friendly, OpenAI integration strong hai
🧠 Chapter 3 — DevOps
  • Docker = App + dependencies = Container (runs everywhere, same behavior)
  • Kubernetes = Containers ko orchestrate karta hai — deploy, scale, heal auto
  • CI/CD = Code push → auto test → auto deploy pipeline
  • GitHub Actions = Free CI/CD, YAML workflows, .github/workflows/
🧠 Chapter 4 — Scalability
  • Load Balancer = Traffic distribute karo multiple servers mein
  • Caching = Pehle compute kiye results store karo (Redis)
  • Horizontal Scaling = Zyada servers add karo (better than vertical)
  • Queue = Async tasks ke liye — user ko instantly respond karo, background mein process karo
🧠 Chapter 5 — Security
  • AuthN = "Tum kaun ho?" (JWT, OAuth), AuthZ = "Tumhara haq kya hai?" (RBAC)
  • Rate Limiting = Abuse rokna (Redis + counter pattern)
  • Secret Management = NEVER hardcode keys — .env / AWS Secrets Manager use karo
  • HTTPS = Always! Let's Encrypt (free), Cloudflare (easier)
🧠 Chapter 6 — Cost Optimization
  • Model Routing = Simple query → Haiku, Complex query → Opus (60x price difference)
  • Prompt Caching = Long system prompts cache karo — 90% savings possible
  • Batch API = Real-time nahi chahiye? 50% discount milta hai
  • Quantization = Self-hosted models 4-bit compress karo — 4x memory savings

🏗 Production Architecture — Complete Picture

Complete Production AI Application Architecture
✅ Production Checklist
  • ☐ HTTPS enabled (Let's Encrypt / Cloudflare)
  • ☐ API keys .env mein / AWS Secrets Manager mein
  • ☐ Rate limiting implemented (per user + global)
  • ☐ JWT authentication working
  • ☐ Docker container build aur tested
  • ☐ CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions)
  • ☐ Monitoring setup (CloudWatch / Grafana)
  • ☐ Error logging (Sentry ya equivalent)
  • ☐ Redis caching for LLM responses
  • ☐ Cost monitoring + alerts setup
🎉
Part 13 Complete!

Tumne Production & Deployment ke saare concepts seekh liye! Ab tum apni AI applications ko professionally deploy, scale, secure aur optimize kar sakte ho. Yeh real-world engineering skills hain jo tumhara package significant increase karengi.

✅ Backend Deployment ✅ Cloud Platforms ✅ DevOps ✅ Scalability ✅ Security ✅ Cost Optimization
